title: scConvert — Developer Notes
text: file captures developer-facing context meant users CRAN reviewers important future maintainers. Normal user-facing changes belong NEWS.md; manuscript-specific analysis lives companion repository scConvert-manuscript/. Last updated: 2026-04-07

what: 1. Recent bug fixes (and their regression tests)
title: scConvert — Developer Notes
text: three bugs discovered preparing Nature Methods / GB submission benchmark suite. matching test tests/testthat/test-regression-fixes.R; one tests fails, fix regressed.

previous_headings: 1. Recent bug fixes (and their regression tests)
title: scConvert — Developer Notes
text: Symptom. readH5AD("pbmc3k_processed.h5ad") (scanpy.datasets.pbmc3k_processed()) used fail return structurally invalid dgCMatrix: column indices within rows monotonically increasing, violating dgCMatrix contract (@must sorted within column defined @p). Root cause. scipy’s CSR/CSC matrices required carry sorted indices. scanpy sometimes ships files state. reinterpret CSR (cells x genes) CSC (genes x cells) readH5AD(), unsorted “column indices within row” become unsorted “row indices within column” dgCMatrix view, validity bug. Fix. LoadH5AD.R:95-148 defines .sort_dgc_indices() () detects whether column unsorted row indices via fast scan, (b) sorts within column . runs sort needed. Mirror fix LoadH5MU.R:94-114. Regression test. test-regression-fixes.R constructs 5x4 CSR deliberately shuffled indices, loads , verifies (1) successful load, (2) correct numerical reconstruction, (3) columns resulting dgCMatrix sorted @. Status. Confirmed fixed; test passes.

previous_headings: 1. Recent bug fixes (and their regression tests)
title: scConvert — Developer Notes
text: Symptom. direct h5Seurat -> Zarr converter (invoked via scConvert("foo.h5seurat", "foo.zarr") standalone H5SeuratToZarr()) used crash dimensionality mismatch error encountering 1D dense HDF5 dataset inside h5Seurat structure. Root cause. 1D dense dataset reliably interpreted (rows, cols) matrix without external shape metadata; converter attempting blind reshape. Fix. R/Convert.R:6489-6493 – warns skips offending dataset rather crashing. caller loses metadata field gets valid Zarr store. Regression test. Tiny Seurat object -> writeH5Seurat -> H5SeuratToZarr -> readZarr; asserts crash ncol/nrow match. Status. Confirmed fixed; test passes. Referenced manuscript Limitations “h5Seurat -> Zarr direct converter dimensionality mismatch bug time initial benchmarking; since fixed regression test included test suite.”

previous_headings: 1. Recent bug fixes (and their regression tests)
title: scConvert — Developer Notes
text: Symptom. writing Seurat object processed ScaleData(), resulting Zarr store read back anndata.read_zarr(): raised ValueError: Value passed key 'scale.data' incorrect shape. Root cause. Seurat::ScaleData() produces matrix shape (n_hvg x n_cells), n_hvg usually much smaller n_genes (default 2000). AnnData requires every entry layers/* match shape X, writing scale.data layer violates AnnData contract. Fix. R/SaveZarr.R:131-133 skips layer whose dimensions match X. Emits informational message. Regression test. Creates 50-gene x 30-cell Seurat object, runs NormalizeData + FindVariableFeatures(nfeatures=20) + ScaleData, confirms dim(scale.data) == (20, 30), calls writeZarr(), asserts () store created, (b) layers/scale.data directory exists, (c) store round-trips readZarr(). Status. Confirmed fixed; test passes.

title: scConvert — Developer Notes
text: appeared “failed” rows task5_spatial.csv error message HDF5-API Errors: file signature found. scConvert bug; dataset download/manifest bug companion benchmark repo. found. files scConvert-manuscript/data/tier2/spatial/stereoseq_mosta.h5ad scConvert-manuscript/data/tier2/spatial/cosmx_nsclc.h5ad HTML documents, HDF5 files. first eight bytes 3c 21 44 4f 43 54 59 50 (“<!DOCTYP”) instead HDF5 magic number 89 48 44 46 0d 0a 1a 0a. cause: original manifest entries pointed HTTP downloader landing pages (https://db.cngb.org/stomics/mosta/download/ https://nanostring.com/.../ffpe-dataset/), direct file URLs. downloader saved HTML response bodies .h5ad extensions without verifying content type. scConvert correct reject . Upstream reality. Stereo-seq native format .gef (HDF5-based Stereo-seq schema AnnData-compatible) .gem (plain text). scverse-compatible path convert via stereopy.io.stereo_to_anndata(). CNGB hosts MOSTA .gef files behind free account. CosMx SMI native format flat-file bundle: cell metadata CSV, expression matrix CSV, FOV positions CSV, tiff stacks. canonical conversion AnnData squidpy.read.nanostring(). NanoString distributes bundles behind gated vendor portal. Fixes applied companion repo (package). benchmark/80_download_datasets.R gains .is_hdf5_file() helper validates HDF5 magic number downloaded file whose declared format HDF5-based. downloader now refuses save non-HDF5 payloads .h5ad extension emits clear diagnostic (“looks like HTML landing page; update URL manifest”). benchmark/00_config.R::check_dataset_availability() also applies signature check, pre-existing corrupt files filtered downstream task runs even re-downloaded. Two new download methods nanostring_convert stereoseq_convert implement squidpy.read.nanostring() stereopy conversion paths, respectively. expect manually-staged raw bundle data/raw/{cosmx,stereoseq}/ upstream sources gated. dataset manifest entries stereoseq_mosta cosmx_nsclc rewritten document point new conversion methods. two corrupt HTML--h5ad files deleted. Manuscript consequence. spatial Results section now honestly notes: “Two additional technologies (Stereo-seq MOSTA CosMx SMI) processed public exemplar files stored HDF5; scConvert therefore evaluated without upstream format converter.” Take-away maintainers. someone adds new spatial multi-vendor benchmark dataset, verify relying () download URL direct file link, landing page, (b) file passes .is_hdf5_file() equivalent signature test declared format.

what: 3. Known limitations that are not bugs
title: scConvert — Developer Notes
text: Several items original limitation list addressed development branch; remains truly format-inherent deferred. Closed 0.1.0.9001 integration pass (2026-04-07): CLI varp preservation. sc_stream_varp() now handles sparse (CSR/CSC group) dense (2D array) varp entries h5ad <-> h5seurat streaming. See src/sc_groups.c CLI block tests/testthat/test-varp-roundtrip.R. IMC multi-image double-roundtrip. R/SpatialConversion.R:245 now sorts Images() iterates every image instead processing Images()[1]. Synthetic regression test tests/testthat/test-imc-images-ordering.R. FOV segmentation/molecules silent loss. WriteFOVToH5AD() ReadFOVFromH5AD() R/SpatialFOV.R serialise FOV boundaries molecules uns/spatial/{library}/segmentation/ molecules/ subgroups. Regression test tests/testthat/test-fov-roundtrip.R. Vendor raw formats (Stereo-seq GEF, CosMx SMI bundles). Read-support lives R/LoadStereoSeq.R R/LoadCosMx.R; C CLI auto-detects delegates R backend via execvp("Rscript"). Dense embedding overflow risk CLI. sc_xmalloc() + sc_check_mul_size() guard zarr/loom transpose allocations src/sc_zarr.c:1043, 1668, 1674 src/sc_loom.c:138. Loom factor-level / h5mu per-modality uns. R/SaveLoom.R R/LoadLoom.R round-trip factor levels via scConvert_extensions/col_factor_levels/{name}. R/Convert.R::writeH5MU R/LoadH5MU.R mirror per-modality uns via misc[["__h5mu_uns_per_mod__"]]. Still open / truly inherent: Non-Visium spatial fidelity remains <100% fields. Command logs proprietary metadata Seurat slot still vanish. FOV fix closes silent-loss gap CosMx/Xenium, IMC panel metadata 4i channel ordering remain format-level problems. C CLI formal memory-safety audit. sc_xmalloc() overflow guards added dominant dense-alloc sites, fuzzer pass (AFL++/libFuzzer malformed h5ad) still planned pre-submission. HDF5 2.0 forward compatibility untested. CLI depends H5Oget_info3 present since HDF5 1.10. HDF5 2.0 released 2026-04-07 test possible. BPCells streaming integration CLI. readH5AD(use.bpcells=TRUE) works R level CLI produce BPCells-compatible output.

Re-run Rscript benchmark/94_audit_manuscript.R companion repo. Direct converter count: Methods claims “36 direct AToB() converter functions”. add remove exports form AToB(), update manuscript. CLI format list: “h5ad, h5Seurat, h5mu, Loom, Zarr”. stay sync src/main.c. Seurat v5 path adaptation: manuscript describes reading assays/{assay}/data (v4) assays/{assay}/layers/data (v5). logic lives R/h5Seurat.R:406-416. audit script scConvert-manuscript/benchmark/94_audit_manuscript.R extracts numbers benchmark CSVs greps manuscript stale markers. exits non-zero known-bad phrase detected. Run make audit companion repo every submission.

title: Atlas-Scale Datasets with BPCells
text: use Cross-tissue immune cell atlas (Domínguez Conde et al., Science 2022), T & innate lymphoid cell subset — 216,611 cells × 35,469 genes across 12 tissues (1.6 GB h5ad). candidates CELLxGENE:
code: # Download T & innate lymphoid cells subset (~1.6 GB) url <- "https://datasets.cellxgene.cziscience.com/6cd641fe-5ee8-47b4-92dc-26816e270d5e.h5ad" atlas_path <- "immune_tcells.h5ad" options(timeout = 600) download.file(url, atlas_path, mode = "wb")

title: Atlas-Scale Datasets with BPCells
text: Without BPCells, full expression matrix loaded RAM sparse matrix. 217K cells × 35K genes, requires ~8.5 GB RAM. 16 distinct immune cell types clearly resolved UMAP. Cells 12 tissues (blood, spleen, bone marrow, thymus, lymph nodes, etc.) intermingle UMAP, confirming shared immune cell identities across tissues.
code: system.time(atlas <- readH5AD("immune_tcells.h5ad")) #>   Cells: 216611 #>   Features: 35469 #>   elapsed: 28.8 s  format(object.size(atlas), units = "GB") #> "8.5 Gb" DimPlot(atlas, group.by = "cell_type", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE,         label.size = 2.5, pt.size = 0.05) +   NoLegend() + ggtitle("217K immune cells — standard load (28.8s)") DimPlot(atlas, group.by = "tissue", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE,         label.size = 3, pt.size = 0.05) +   NoLegend() + ggtitle("Colored by tissue of origin")

title: Atlas-Scale Datasets with BPCells
text: use.bpcells, expression matrix stays disk. metadata, embeddings, graphs loaded RAM.

title: Atlas-Scale Datasets with BPCells
text: Pass use.bpcells = TRUE keep matrix backed original h5ad file. data copied. UMAP identical — BPCells changes storage, values.
code: system.time(atlas_bp <- readH5AD("immune_tcells.h5ad", use.bpcells = TRUE)) #>   elapsed: 12.6 s  (2.3x faster)  format(object.size(atlas_bp), units = "MB") #> "169.2 Mb"  (vs 8.5 GB — 98% reduction) DimPlot(atlas_bp, group.by = "cell_type", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE,         label.size = 2.5, pt.size = 0.05) +   NoLegend() + ggtitle("217K cells — BPCells on-disk (12.6s)")

title: Atlas-Scale Datasets with BPCells
text: Pass directory path convert BPCells’ optimized bitpacked format.
code: # First load writes the cache system.time(atlas_bp2 <- readH5AD("immune_tcells.h5ad", use.bpcells = "bp_cache")) #>   elapsed: 13.5 s  # Subsequent loads reuse the cache system.time(atlas_bp2 <- readH5AD("immune_tcells.h5ad", use.bpcells = "bp_cache")) #>   elapsed: 13.2 s

title: Atlas-Scale Datasets with BPCells
text: Measured 217K-cell cross-tissue immune atlas (35K genes, 1.6 GB h5ad). Apple M4 Max, 128 GB RAM. BPCells reduces memory 98% expression matrix (dominates object size ~8.3 GB) never materialized R’s memory. metadata (~25 columns × 217K rows) UMAP embedding (217K × 2) loaded.

title: Atlas-Scale Datasets with BPCells
text: Verify BPCells mode works system small shipped demo.
code:
has_bp <- requireNamespace("BPCells", quietly = TRUE) h5ad_file <- system.file("extdata", "pbmc_demo.h5ad", package = "scConvert")  if (has_bp) {   bp_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "bpcells_demo")    obj_bp <- readH5AD(h5ad_file, use.bpcells = bp_dir, verbose = FALSE)   obj_std <- readH5AD(h5ad_file, verbose = FALSE)    cat("Standard object size:", format(object.size(obj_std), units = "KB"), "
")   cat("BPCells object size: ", format(object.size(obj_bp), units = "KB"), "
")   cat("Matrix class (standard):", class(GetAssayData(obj_std, layer = "counts"))[1], "
")   cat("Matrix class (BPCells): ", class(GetAssayData(obj_bp, layer = "counts"))[1], "
")    unlink(bp_dir, recursive = TRUE) } else {   cat("BPCells not installed. Install with:
")   cat('  remotes::install_github("bnprks/BPCells/r")
') } #> Warning: Matrix compression performs poorly with non-integers. #> • Consider calling convert_matrix_type if a compressed integer matrix is intended. #> This message is displayed once every 8 hours. #> Warning: Layer 'data' is empty #> Standard object size: 3377.9 Kb  #> BPCells object size:  1362.6 Kb  #> Matrix class (standard): dgCMatrix  #> Matrix class (BPCells):  RenameDims if (has_bp) {   library(patchwork)   p1 <- DimPlot(obj_std, group.by = "seurat_annotations", label = TRUE, pt.size = 1) +     ggtitle("Standard (in-memory)") + NoLegend()   p2 <- DimPlot(obj_bp, group.by = "seurat_annotations", label = TRUE, pt.size = 1) +     ggtitle("BPCells (on-disk)") + NoLegend()   p1 + p2 }

title: Atlas-Scale Datasets with BPCells
text: HDF5 format pairs, C binary converts directly without loading R:
code: # Convert the 217K-cell atlas without loading into R scConvert_cli("immune_tcells.h5ad", "immune_tcells.h5seurat") #>   elapsed: 55.5 s  (1.6 GB file)  # Or use the one-liner (auto-detects the fastest path) scConvert("immune_tcells.h5ad", dest = "h5seurat")

what: Subset and export after BPCells loading
title: Atlas-Scale Datasets with BPCells
text: Load BPCells, filter cell type interest, save:
code: atlas <- readH5AD("immune_tcells.h5ad", use.bpcells = TRUE)  # Subset to regulatory T cells only tregs <- subset(atlas, cell_type == "regulatory T cell") cat("Tregs:", ncol(tregs), "cells\n") #> Tregs: 6842 cells  writeH5AD(tregs, "tregs_only.h5ad") saveRDS(tregs, "tregs_only.rds")

title: Command-Line Interface
text: scConvert includes standalone C binary streaming HDF5 conversions h5ad, h5Seurat, h5mu, Loom – R Python runtime required. formats need R (RDS, Zarr, SCE, SOMA), scConvert_cli() wrapper falls back R hub automatically.

title: Command-Line Interface
text: Load bundled PBMC demo data (500 cells, 9 cell types) save h5Seurat – starting point CLI conversion.
code: obj <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "pbmc_demo.rds", package = "scConvert")) DimPlot(obj, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_annotations") +   ggplot2::ggtitle("PBMC demo (500 cells)") h5s_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".h5Seurat") writeH5Seurat(obj, h5s_path, overwrite = TRUE, verbose = FALSE) cat("Saved:", h5s_path, "\n") #> Saved: /tmp/RtmpFnxh5q/file467f4c0ca9bd.h5Seurat

what: Using the C binary from the shell
title: Command-Line Interface
text: binary auto-detects conversion direction file extensions. Options:
code: # Build (one time) cd /path/to/scConvert/src && make  # Convert scconvert data.h5seurat data.h5ad scconvert data.h5ad data.h5seurat --assay RNA --gzip 6 scconvert multimodal.h5mu output.h5seurat scconvert data.h5ad data.loom

title: Command-Line Interface
text: R wrapper tries C binary first, falls back R streaming Seurat hub. works even binary compiled. Verify round-tripped data intact:
code:
h5ad_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".h5ad") scConvert_cli(h5s_path, h5ad_path, verbose = FALSE) #> Validating h5Seurat file #> [1] TRUE cat("Converted to:", h5ad_path, "
") #> Converted to: /tmp/RtmpFnxh5q/file467f654f94af.h5ad obj_rt <- readH5AD(h5ad_path, verbose = FALSE) #> Warning: Layer 'data' is empty cat("Cells:", ncol(obj_rt), "| Genes:", nrow(obj_rt), "
") #> Cells: 500 | Genes: 2000 cat("Reductions:", paste(names(obj_rt@reductions), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Reductions: pca, umap  FeaturePlot(obj_rt, features = "LYZ", reduction = "umap") +   ggtitle("LYZ expression after CLI round-trip")

title: Command-Line Interface
text: Convert h5ad files directory: formats supported C binary (RDS, Zarr, SCE), function works via R hub:
code: h5ad_files <- list.files(".", pattern = "\\.h5ad$", full.names = TRUE) for (f in h5ad_files) {   out <- sub("\\.h5ad$", ".h5seurat", f)   scConvert_cli(f, out) } scConvert_cli("data.h5ad", "data.rds") scConvert_cli("data.rds", "data.zarr") scConvert_cli("data.h5ad", "data.zarr")

title: Command-Line Interface
text: C binary uses direct chunk copy sparse zero-copy avoid decompressing data. Median wall-clock seconds synthetic sparse h5ad (20K genes, 5% density, Apple M4 Max): CLI 10–50x faster never constructs Seurat object. loading data R analysis, use readH5AD() readH5Seurat().

title: Command-Line Interface
text: binary optional – scConvert_cli() works without . binary links libhdf5 libz . dependencies.
code: # macOS (Homebrew) brew install hdf5 cd /path/to/scConvert/src && make  # Ubuntu/Debian sudo apt-get install libhdf5-dev cd /path/to/scConvert/src && make  # Copy to PATH cp src/scconvert ~/bin/

title: In-Memory vs On-Disk Conversion
code: obj <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "pbmc_demo.rds", package = "scConvert")) DimPlot(obj, group.by = "seurat_annotations", label = TRUE, pt.size = 1) +   ggtitle("500 PBMCs — demo dataset") + NoLegend()

title: In-Memory vs On-Disk Conversion
text: default mode loads data Seurat object, saves target format. supports format pairs requires full dataset RAM. Use -memory conversion need manipulate data R, source/destination formats don’t share -disk layout.
code: # Seurat object -> h5ad (in-memory) h5ad_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc_hub.h5ad") t1 <- system.time(writeH5AD(obj, h5ad_path, verbose = FALSE)) cat("In-memory write:", round(t1["elapsed"], 2), "s\n") #> In-memory write: 2.97 s

title: In-Memory vs On-Disk Conversion
text: Zarr conversions, scConvert can copy data field--field files without ever constructing Seurat object. keeps memory usage constant regardless dataset size. Available streaming converters: accept stream = TRUE (default) bypass Seurat intermediate.
code: # h5ad -> zarr (streaming, no Seurat object in memory) zarr_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc_stream.zarr") t2 <- system.time(H5ADToZarr(h5ad_path, zarr_path, stream = TRUE, verbose = FALSE)) cat("Streaming h5ad -> zarr:", round(t2["elapsed"], 2), "s\n") #> Streaming h5ad -> zarr: 0.97 s

title: In-Memory vs On-Disk Conversion
text: HDF5-based format pairs (h5ad, h5Seurat, h5mu, Loom), compiled C binary copies datasets directly HDF5 level. uses constant memory typically 10–50x faster R path. Build binary cd src && make (requires HDF5 headers).
code: h5s_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc_cli.h5seurat") writeH5Seurat(obj, h5s_path, overwrite = TRUE, verbose = FALSE)  h5ad_cli <- file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc_cli.h5ad") t3 <- system.time(scConvert_cli(h5s_path, h5ad_cli, verbose = FALSE)) #> Validating h5Seurat file cat("C binary h5seurat -> h5ad:", round(t3["elapsed"], 2), "s\n") #> C binary h5seurat -> h5ad: 4.02 s

title: In-Memory vs On-Disk Conversion
text: synthetic sparse h5ad files (20K genes, 5% density), median 3 runs Apple M4 Max: C binary stays 1 second even 500K cells never decompresses expression matrix — copies HDF5 chunks directly.

title: In-Memory vs On-Disk Conversion
text: Seurat object can saved several R-native formats. preserve full object structure (assays, reductions, graphs, metadata, images).
code: rds_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc.rds") h5s_path2 <- file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc.h5seurat")  saveRDS(obj, rds_path) writeH5Seurat(obj, h5s_path2, overwrite = TRUE, verbose = FALSE)  sizes <- data.frame(   Format = character(), Size_KB = numeric(), stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) sizes <- rbind(sizes, data.frame(Format = "RDS (.rds)", Size_KB = file.size(rds_path) / 1024)) sizes <- rbind(sizes, data.frame(Format = "h5Seurat (.h5seurat)", Size_KB = file.size(h5s_path2) / 1024))  if (requireNamespace("qs2", quietly = TRUE)) {   qs2_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc.qs2")   qs2::qs_save(obj, qs2_path)   sizes <- rbind(sizes, data.frame(Format = "qs2 (.qs2)", Size_KB = file.size(qs2_path) / 1024)) }  # Also save as RData rdata_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc.RData") save(obj, file = rdata_path) sizes <- rbind(sizes, data.frame(Format = "RData (.RData)", Size_KB = file.size(rdata_path) / 1024))  sizes$Size_MB <- round(sizes$Size_KB / 1024, 2) sizes$Size_KB <- round(sizes$Size_KB, 0) knitr::kable(sizes[, c("Format", "Size_MB")], col.names = c("Format", "Size (MB)"))

title: In-Memory vs On-Disk Conversion
text: Key differences: qs2 among fastest R serialization formats (2–5x faster saveRDS), similar better compression. Use local caching. original qs package archived CRAN 2026 superseded qs2. h5Seurat format supports selective loading — read just one assay one reduction without loading entire object. RDS / RData universally available slower large objects.

title: In-Memory vs On-Disk Conversion
text: sharing data Python tools, use cross-language formats:
code: h5ad_path2 <- file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc_exchange.h5ad") writeH5AD(obj, h5ad_path2, verbose = FALSE)  loom_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc_exchange.loom") writeLoom(obj, loom_path, verbose = FALSE) #> Adding col attribute CellID #> Adding col attribute orig.ident #> Adding col attribute nCount_RNA #> Adding col attribute nFeature_RNA #> Adding col attribute seurat_annotations #> Adding col attribute percent.mt #> Adding col attribute RNA_snn_res.0.5 #> Adding col attribute seurat_clusters #> Adding row attribute Gene #> Adding row attribute vst.mean #> Adding row attribute vst.variance #> Adding row attribute vst.variance.expected #> Adding row attribute vst.variance.standardized #> Adding row attribute vst.variable  zarr_path2 <- file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc_exchange.zarr") writeZarr(obj, zarr_path2, verbose = FALSE)  exchange <- data.frame(   Format = c("h5ad (AnnData)", "Loom", "Zarr"),   Size_MB = round(c(     file.size(h5ad_path2),     file.size(loom_path),     sum(file.info(list.files(zarr_path2, recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE))$size)   ) / 1024^2, 2),   Ecosystem = c("scanpy, CELLxGENE", "loompy, velocyto", "cloud / AnnData") ) knitr::kable(exchange)

title: Convert Between Seurat and AnnData (h5ad)
text: scConvert ships 500-cell PBMC dataset h5ad format. readH5AD() loads directly Seurat object – intermediate conversion step needed.
code: h5ad_file <- system.file("extdata", "pbmc_demo.h5ad", package = "scConvert") pbmc <- readH5AD(h5ad_file) pbmc #> An object of class Seurat  #> 2000 features across 500 samples within 1 assay  #> Active assay: RNA (2000 features, 2000 variable features) #>  2 layers present: counts, data #>  2 dimensional reductions calculated: pca, umap

title: Convert Between Seurat and AnnData (h5ad)
text: reader reconstructs full Seurat object h5ad contents, including metadata, dimensional reductions, neighbor graphs. nine annotated cell types preserved factor column:  can also visualize marker gene. LYZ highly expressed monocytes:
code:
cat("Cells:", ncol(pbmc), "
") #> Cells: 500 cat("Genes:", nrow(pbmc), "
") #> Genes: 2000 cat("Reductions:", paste(names(pbmc@reductions), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Reductions: pca, umap cat("Metadata columns:", paste(colnames(pbmc[[]]), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Metadata columns: orig.ident, nCount_RNA, nFeature_RNA, seurat_annotations, percent.mt, RNA_snn_res.0.5, seurat_clusters DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_annotations",         label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.5) + NoLegend() FeaturePlot(pbmc, features = "LYZ", pt.size = 0.5)

what: Write a Seurat object to h5ad
title: Convert Between Seurat and AnnData (h5ad)
text: go direction, load Seurat object write h5ad. normalized data matrix goes X, raw counts go raw/X, metadata, reductions, graphs preserved.
code: pbmc_seurat <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "pbmc_demo.rds", package = "scConvert")) h5ad_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".h5ad") writeH5AD(pbmc_seurat, h5ad_path, overwrite = TRUE) cat("h5ad file size:", round(file.size(h5ad_path) / 1e6, 1), "MB\n") #> h5ad file size: 0.9 MB

title: Convert Between Seurat and AnnData (h5ad)
text: Read newly written h5ad back confirm expression values, cluster labels, UMAP coordinates preserved.
code: pbmc_rt <- readH5AD(h5ad_path) library(patchwork) p1 <- DimPlot(pbmc_seurat, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_annotations",               label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.5) + NoLegend() + ggtitle("Original (.rds)") p2 <- DimPlot(pbmc_rt, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_annotations",               label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.5) + NoLegend() + ggtitle("Round-trip (.h5ad)") p1 + p2

title: Convert Between Seurat and AnnData (h5ad)
text: quick format conversion without loading data R, use scConvert() dispatcher. detects source destination formats file extensions picks efficient conversion path:
code: h5seurat_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".h5seurat") scConvert(h5ad_path, dest = h5seurat_path, overwrite = TRUE) cat("Converted h5ad to h5Seurat:", round(file.size(h5seurat_path) / 1e6, 1), "MB\n") #> Converted h5ad to h5Seurat: 1 MB

title: Convert Between Seurat and AnnData (h5ad)
text: conversion, scConvert maps data Seurat h5ad follows:

title: Convert Between Seurat and AnnData (h5ad)
text: h5ad files produced writeH5AD() fully compatible scanpy, scvi-tools, CELLxGENE. Python scanpy installed:
code: import scanpy as sc  adata = sc.read_h5ad("pbmc_demo.h5ad") print(adata)  # Visualize with cluster annotations sc.pl.umap(adata, color="seurat_annotations")  # Expression patterns are preserved sc.pl.umap(adata, color="LYZ", use_raw=False)

title: Convert Between Seurat and AnnData (h5ad)
code: unlink(h5ad_path) unlink(h5seurat_path)

title: Convert to Loom Format
text: scConvert ships 500-cell PBMC dataset Loom format. readLoom() loads directly Seurat object. reader reconstructs cell metadata, gene metadata, dimensional reductions Loom column row attributes: nine annotated cell types preserved:  Expression data fully available. LYZ monocyte marker:
code:
loom_file <- system.file("extdata", "pbmc_demo.loom", package = "scConvert") pbmc <- readLoom(loom_file) pbmc #> An object of class Seurat  #> 2000 features across 500 samples within 1 assay  #> Active assay: RNA (2000 features, 0 variable features) #>  2 layers present: counts, data #>  1 dimensional reduction calculated: umap cat("Cells:", ncol(pbmc), "
") #> Cells: 500 cat("Genes:", nrow(pbmc), "
") #> Genes: 2000 cat("Reductions:", paste(names(pbmc@reductions), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Reductions: umap cat("Metadata columns:", paste(colnames(pbmc[[]]), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Metadata columns: orig.ident, nCount_RNA, nFeature_RNA, RNA_snn_res.0.5, percent.mt, seurat_annotations, seurat_clusters DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_annotations",         label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.5) + NoLegend() FeaturePlot(pbmc, features = "LYZ", pt.size = 0.5)

what: Write a Seurat object to Loom
title: Convert to Loom Format
text: writeLoom() saves default assay’s expression data main matrix, cell metadata column attributes gene metadata row attributes. Dimensional reductions stored additional column attributes.
code: pbmc_seurat <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "pbmc_demo.rds", package = "scConvert")) loom_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".loom") writeLoom(pbmc_seurat, filename = loom_path, overwrite = TRUE) cat("Loom file size:", round(file.size(loom_path) / 1e6, 1), "MB\n") #> Loom file size: 2.3 MB

title: Convert to Loom Format
text: Read written Loom file back compare UMAP projections.
code: pbmc_rt <- readLoom(loom_path) library(patchwork) p1 <- DimPlot(pbmc_seurat, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_annotations",               label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.5) + NoLegend() + ggtitle("Original (.rds)") p2 <- DimPlot(pbmc_rt, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_annotations",               label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.5) + NoLegend() + ggtitle("Round-trip (.loom)") p1 + p2

title: Convert to Loom Format
text: Loom simpler format h5ad h5Seurat. summary round-trips :

title: Convert to Loom Format
text: violin plot confirms per-cluster expression distributions preserved Loom conversion:
code: VlnPlot(pbmc_rt, features = "LYZ", group.by = "seurat_annotations", pt.size = 0) +   NoLegend()

title: Convert to Loom Format
text: Loom files produced writeLoom() compatible loompy, scanpy, scVelo.
code: import loompy  with loompy.connect("pbmc_demo.loom") as ds:     print(f"Shape: {ds.shape[0]} genes x {ds.shape[1]} cells")     print(f"Row attributes: {list(ds.ra.keys())}")     print(f"Column attributes: {list(ds.ca.keys())[:10]}")     print(f"Layers: {list(ds.layers.keys())}") import scanpy as sc  adata = sc.read_loom("pbmc_demo.loom", sparse=True, cleanup=False) print(adata)

title: Convert to Loom Format
code: unlink(loom_path)

title: Convert Between Seurat and SingleCellExperiment
text: scConvert ships small PBMC dataset. start reading Seurat object.
code: pbmc <- readH5AD(system.file("extdata", "pbmc_demo.h5ad", package = "scConvert")) pbmc #> An object of class Seurat  #> 2000 features across 500 samples within 1 assay  #> Active assay: RNA (2000 features, 2000 variable features) #>  2 layers present: counts, data #>  2 dimensional reductions calculated: pca, umap

title: Convert Between Seurat and SingleCellExperiment
text: Convert Seurat object SCE. Counts, normalized data, dimensional reductions, cell metadata transferred. Inspect carried :
code:
library(SingleCellExperiment)  sce <- as.SingleCellExperiment(pbmc) sce #> class: SingleCellExperiment  #> dim: 2000 500  #> metadata(0): #> assays(2): counts logcounts #> rownames(2000): PPBP LYZ ... CLIC2 HEMGN #> rowData names(0): #> colnames(500): AAACATTGAGCTAC AAACATTGATCAGC ... TTTCGAACTCTCAT #>   TTTGCATGCCTCAC #> colData names(8): orig.ident nCount_RNA ... seurat_clusters ident #> reducedDimNames(2): PCA UMAP #> mainExpName: RNA #> altExpNames(0): cat("Assays:", paste(assayNames(sce), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Assays: counts, logcounts cat("Reduced dims:", paste(reducedDimNames(sce), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Reduced dims: PCA, UMAP cat("colData columns:", paste(colnames(colData(sce)), collapse = ", "), "
") #> colData columns: orig.ident, nCount_RNA, nFeature_RNA, seurat_annotations, percent.mt, RNA_snn_res.0.5, seurat_clusters, ident

title: Convert Between Seurat and SingleCellExperiment
text: Convert SCE back Seurat object .Seurat(). converter detects assays exist (counts, logcounts) maps appropriate Seurat layers.
code: seurat_rt <- as.Seurat(sce, counts = "counts", data = "logcounts") seurat_rt #> An object of class Seurat  #> 2000 features across 500 samples within 1 assay  #> Active assay: RNA (2000 features, 0 variable features) #>  2 layers present: counts, data #>  2 dimensional reductions calculated: PCA, UMAP

title: Convert Between Seurat and SingleCellExperiment
text: Confirm expression values, cell barcodes, embeddings survive Seurat -> SCE -> Seurat round-trip.
code:
# Cell barcodes cat("Barcodes match:", identical(Cells(pbmc), Cells(seurat_rt)), "
") #> Barcodes match: TRUE  # Gene names cat("Features match:", identical(rownames(pbmc), rownames(seurat_rt)), "
") #> Features match: TRUE  # Count matrix values orig_counts <- GetAssayData(pbmc, layer = "counts") rt_counts <- GetAssayData(seurat_rt, layer = "counts") cat("Counts identical:", identical(orig_counts, rt_counts), "
") #> Counts identical: TRUE  # Dimensionality reductions (SCE stores these in reducedDims) cat("Reductions in original:", paste(names(pbmc@reductions), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Reductions in original: pca, umap cat("Reductions after round-trip:", paste(names(seurat_rt@reductions), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Reductions after round-trip: PCA, UMAP

title: Convert Between Seurat and SingleCellExperiment
text: share SCE dataset Python collaborators, pass directly scConvert(). SCE converted Seurat internally, written target format – manual intermediate step needed. Read back confirm data survived full SCE -> Seurat -> h5ad -> Seurat chain:
code:
h5ad_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".h5ad") scConvert(sce, dest = h5ad_path, overwrite = TRUE) cat("h5ad file size:", round(file.size(h5ad_path) / 1e6, 1), "MB
") #> h5ad file size: 0.9 MB pbmc_h5ad <- readH5AD(h5ad_path) cat("Cells:", ncol(pbmc_h5ad), "
") #> Cells: 500 cat("Genes:", nrow(pbmc_h5ad), "
") #> Genes: 2000 cat("Reductions:", paste(names(pbmc_h5ad@reductions), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Reductions: PCA, UMAP

title: Convert Between Seurat and SingleCellExperiment
text: reverse also works. Use dest = "sce" get SingleCellExperiment directly Seurat object:
code: sce2 <- scConvert(pbmc, dest = "sce") cat("Class:", class(sce2), "\n") #> Class: SingleCellExperiment cat("Dimensions:", nrow(sce2), "x", ncol(sce2), "\n") #> Dimensions: 2000 x 500

what: Practical example: Bioconductor QC on SCE, then back to Seurat
title: Convert Between Seurat and SingleCellExperiment
text: common workflow use Bioconductor tools quality control normalization, bring result back Seurat downstream analysis. compute log-library-size detected-feature counts using base R (quantities scater::addPerCellQCMetrics compute). new QC columns (log_library_size, detected_features) now available Seurat metadata filtering visualization.
code: # Compute QC metrics on the SCE sce_qc <- as.SingleCellExperiment(pbmc) counts_mat <- assay(sce_qc, "counts") colData(sce_qc)$log_library_size <- log1p(Matrix::colSums(counts_mat)) colData(sce_qc)$detected_features <- Matrix::colSums(counts_mat > 0)  cat("Added QC columns:",     paste(c("log_library_size", "detected_features"), collapse = ", "), "\n") #> Added QC columns: log_library_size, detected_features  # Convert back to Seurat -- the new colData columns become metadata seurat_qc <- as.Seurat(sce_qc, counts = "counts", data = "logcounts") cat("Seurat metadata columns:",     paste(colnames(seurat_qc[[]]), collapse = ", "), "\n") #> Seurat metadata columns: orig.ident, nCount_RNA, nFeature_RNA, seurat_annotations, percent.mt, RNA_snn_res.0.5, seurat_clusters, ident, log_library_size, detected_features

title: Convert Between Seurat and SingleCellExperiment
code: unlink(h5ad_path)

title: TileDB-SOMA and CELLxGENE Census
text: TileDB-SOMA cloud-native, array-based format single-cell data. storage backend behind CELLxGENE Census, hosts 61M+ cells across 900+ datasets. SOMA supports efficient slicing cells features without loading full dataset, making ideal working large atlases. scConvert provides writeSOMA() readSOMA() Seurat interoperability. functions require tiledbsoma R package. SOMA experiments database-like stores rather single files, easily shipped package demo data. Instead, start shipped RDS demonstrate round-trip.

title: TileDB-SOMA and CELLxGENE Census
text: use 500-cell PBMC dataset ships scConvert. 9 annotated cell types, PCA/UMAP embeddings, neighbor graphs.
code: pbmc <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "pbmc_demo.rds", package = "scConvert")) pbmc #> An object of class Seurat  #> 2000 features across 500 samples within 1 assay  #> Active assay: RNA (2000 features, 2000 variable features) #>  2 layers present: counts, data #>  2 dimensional reductions calculated: pca, umap DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_annotations",         label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.8) +   ggtitle("PBMC demo -- 9 cell types") + NoLegend()

title: TileDB-SOMA and CELLxGENE Census
text: Save Seurat object SOMA experiment. tiledbsoma installed, section shows code without running .
code: soma_uri <- file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc_demo.soma")  # Ensure graphs have a valid DefaultAssay (required by tiledbsoma) for (gn in names(pbmc@graphs)) {   if (length(DefaultAssay(pbmc@graphs[[gn]])) == 0) {     DefaultAssay(pbmc@graphs[[gn]]) <- DefaultAssay(pbmc)   } }  writeSOMA(pbmc, uri = soma_uri, overwrite = TRUE) cat("SOMA experiment written to:", soma_uri, "\n")

title: TileDB-SOMA and CELLxGENE Census
code: pbmc_rt <- readSOMA(soma_uri) cat("Cells:", ncol(pbmc_rt), "| Genes:", nrow(pbmc_rt), "\n") cat("Metadata columns:", paste(colnames(pbmc_rt[[]]), collapse = ", "), "\n")

title: TileDB-SOMA and CELLxGENE Census
text: Side--side UMAP plots confirm cluster labels coordinates survive SOMA round-trip.
code: library(patchwork)  p1 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_annotations",               label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.8) +   ggtitle("Original") + NoLegend()  p2 <- DimPlot(pbmc_rt, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_annotations",               label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.8) +   ggtitle("After SOMA round-trip") + NoLegend()  p1 + p2

title: TileDB-SOMA and CELLxGENE Census
text: LYZ strong monocyte marker – violin plot shows expression distribution across 9 cell types loaded SOMA store.
code: Idents(pbmc_rt) <- "seurat_annotations" VlnPlot(pbmc_rt, features = "LYZ", pt.size = 0) +   ggtitle("LYZ expression by cell type (from SOMA)") + NoLegend()

title: TileDB-SOMA and CELLxGENE Census
code: stopifnot(ncol(pbmc_rt) == ncol(pbmc)) stopifnot(nrow(pbmc_rt) == nrow(pbmc)) cat("Dimensions match:", ncol(pbmc_rt), "cells x", nrow(pbmc_rt), "genes\n")  shared_meta <- intersect(colnames(pbmc[[]]), colnames(pbmc_rt[[]])) cat("Shared metadata columns:", length(shared_meta), "\n")

title: TileDB-SOMA and CELLxGENE Census
text: CELLxGENE Census stores largest public atlas single-cell data single SOMA experiment. can query readSOMA() convert result format.
code: library(cellxgene.census)  census <- open_soma(census_version = "stable") human_uri <- census$get("census_data")$get("homo_sapiens")$uri  # Read a subset directly as a Seurat object tcells <- readSOMA(   uri = human_uri,   measurement = "RNA",   obs_query = "cell_type == 'T cell' & tissue_general == 'blood'" )  # Save locally in any format writeH5AD(tcells, "census_tcells.h5ad") saveRDS(tcells, "census_tcells.rds")

title: TileDB-SOMA and CELLxGENE Census
text: Direct conversion functions available supported format pairs:
code: # SOMA <-> h5ad H5ADToSOMA("data.h5ad", "data.soma") SOMAToH5AD("data.soma", "data.h5ad")  # SOMA <-> Zarr ZarrToSOMA("data.zarr", "data.soma") SOMAToZarr("data.soma", "data.zarr")  # Or use the universal dispatcher scConvert("data.h5ad", dest = "data.soma", overwrite = TRUE)

title: SpatialData Zarr Stores
text: SpatialData scverse standard spatial omics data. .spatialdata.zarr store combines expression tables, spatial coordinates, shapes, tissue images single Zarr directory. Technologies like Visium, MERFISH, Xenium, Slide-seq, CODEX SpatialData representations, making unifying format spatial transcriptomics ecosystem. scConvert provides readSpatialData() writeSpatialData() move SpatialData stores Seurat objects, Python dependency.

title: SpatialData Zarr Stores
text: start reading 400-spot Visium mouse brain dataset shipped scConvert h5ad file. dataset includes spatial coordinates, tissue image, PCA/UMAP embeddings, 15 clusters across 1500 genes.  Ttr (transthyretin) marks choroid plexus. spatial expression pattern confirms data loaded correctly h5ad file.
code: h5ad_path <- system.file("extdata", "spatial_demo.h5ad", package = "scConvert") brain <- readH5AD(h5ad_path, verbose = FALSE) brain #> An object of class Seurat  #> 1500 features across 400 samples within 1 assay  #> Active assay: RNA (1500 features, 1500 variable features) #>  2 layers present: counts, data #>  2 dimensional reductions calculated: pca, umap #>  1 spatial field of view present: anterior1 SpatialFeaturePlot(brain, features = "Ttr") +   ggtitle("Ttr expression -- Visium mouse brain (from h5ad)") DimPlot(brain, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE, pt.size = 1) +   ggtitle("Cluster assignments (from h5ad)") + NoLegend()

what: Write to SpatialData and read back
title: SpatialData Zarr Stores
text: SpatialData stores complex directory structure separate groups tables, shapes, images, coordinate systems. write Seurat object SpatialData Zarr store read back verify round-trip.
code:
sd_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "brain.spatialdata.zarr") writeSpatialData(brain, sd_path, overwrite = TRUE, verbose = FALSE) cat("SpatialData store written to:", sd_path, "
") #> SpatialData store written to: /tmp/RtmpsLVqa3/brain.spatialdata.zarr brain_rt <- readSpatialData(sd_path, verbose = FALSE) cat("Cells:", ncol(brain_rt), "| Genes:", nrow(brain_rt), "
") #> Cells: 400 | Genes: 1500 cat("Reductions:", paste(names(brain_rt@reductions), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Reductions: pca, umap cat("Images:", paste(names(brain_rt@images), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Images: anterior1

title: SpatialData Zarr Stores
code: library(patchwork)  p1 <- FeaturePlot(brain, features = "Ttr", pt.size = 1) +   ggtitle("Ttr -- Original (h5ad)") p2 <- FeaturePlot(brain_rt, features = "Ttr", pt.size = 1) +   ggtitle("Ttr -- After SpatialData round-trip") p1 + p2

title: SpatialData Zarr Stores
code: stopifnot(ncol(brain_rt) == ncol(brain)) stopifnot(nrow(brain_rt) == nrow(brain)) stopifnot(identical(sort(colnames(brain_rt)), sort(colnames(brain)))) cat("All checks passed:", ncol(brain_rt), "spots x", nrow(brain_rt), "genes.\n") #> All checks passed: 400 spots x 1500 genes.

title: SpatialData Zarr Stores
text: Convert SpatialData formats single call:
code: # SpatialData <-> h5ad SpatialDataToH5AD("sample.spatialdata.zarr", "sample.h5ad") H5ADToSpatialData("sample.h5ad", "sample.spatialdata.zarr")  # SpatialData <-> h5Seurat SpatialDataToH5Seurat("sample.spatialdata.zarr", "sample.h5seurat") H5SeuratToSpatialData("sample.h5seurat", "sample.spatialdata.zarr")  # Or use the universal dispatcher scConvert("sample.spatialdata.zarr", dest = "sample.h5ad", overwrite = TRUE)

title: SpatialData Zarr Stores
text: Images: Large OME-NGFF images (>100M pixels) skipped avoid memory issues. Use images = FALSE read coordinates . Coordinate transforms: SpatialData supports affine transformations elements. currently applied reading; coordinates used -. Labels/segmentation: Cell nucleus segmentation masks recorded name loaded (natural Seurat representation). Multiple tables: one table read per call. Use table argument select one.

title: SpatialData Zarr Stores
text: SpatialData stores written scConvert readable Python spatialdata library. Requires Python spatialdata installed.
code: import spatialdata as sd  sdata = sd.read_zarr("brain.spatialdata.zarr") print(sdata) sdata.pl.render_shapes().pl.show()

title: Convert to Zarr Format
text: Zarr directory-based format chunked, compressed arrays. widely used cloud-native single-cell workflows – including CELLxGENE Census SpatialData – chunk independent file can read parallel object stores like S3 GCS. scConvert reads writes Zarr v2 stores following AnnData -disk specification, Python dependency.

title: Convert to Zarr Format
text: start shipped PBMC h5ad demo convert Zarr can demonstrate Zarr read/write workflow. dataset 230 cells, 220 genes, PCA/UMAP embeddings, neighbor graphs, 9 annotated cell types.  LYZ strong monocyte marker. can verify expression values loaded correctly Zarr store.
code: h5ad_path <- system.file("testdata", "pbmc_small.h5ad", package = "scConvert") zarr_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc_demo.zarr") scConvert(h5ad_path, zarr_path, overwrite = TRUE, verbose = FALSE) pbmc <- readZarr(zarr_path, verbose = FALSE) pbmc #> An object of class Seurat  #> 2000 features across 214 samples within 1 assay  #> Active assay: RNA (2000 features, 2000 variable features) #>  2 layers present: counts, data #>  2 dimensional reductions calculated: pca, umap DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_annotations",         label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.8) +   ggtitle("PBMC data read from Zarr") + NoLegend() FeaturePlot(pbmc, features = "LYZ", pt.size = 0.8) +   ggtitle("LYZ expression (from Zarr)")

what: Write to Zarr and read back
title: Convert to Zarr Format
text: Now write Seurat object new Zarr store read back verify round-trip fidelity.
code: rt_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc_roundtrip.zarr") writeZarr(pbmc, rt_path, overwrite = TRUE, verbose = FALSE) pbmc_rt <- readZarr(rt_path, verbose = FALSE) cat("Cells:", ncol(pbmc_rt), "| Genes:", nrow(pbmc_rt), "\n") #> Cells: 214 | Genes: 2000 cat("Reductions:", paste(names(pbmc_rt@reductions), collapse = ", "), "\n") #> Reductions: pca, umap

title: Convert to Zarr Format
text: converting file formats without loading data R, scConvert provides streaming converters copy fields directly backends: particularly useful large datasets materializing full Seurat object expensive.
code: # h5ad <-> Zarr (no Seurat intermediate) H5ADToZarr("data.h5ad", "data.zarr") ZarrToH5AD("data.zarr", "data.h5ad")  # h5Seurat <-> Zarr H5SeuratToZarr("data.h5seurat", "data.zarr") ZarrToH5Seurat("data.zarr", "data.h5seurat")  # Or use the universal dispatcher scConvert("data.h5ad", dest = "data.zarr", overwrite = TRUE)

title: Convert to Zarr Format
text: Zarr stores produced writeZarr() directly readable Python’s anndata.read_zarr() scanpy. Requires Python anndata installed.
code: import anndata as ad import scanpy as sc  adata = ad.read_zarr("pbmc.zarr") print(adata) sc.pl.umap(adata, color="seurat_annotations")

title: Format Mapping Reference
text: scConvert routes conversions Seurat universal intermediate. reference documents Seurat components map supported file format.

title: Format Mapping Reference
code:
obj <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "pbmc_demo.rds", package = "scConvert"))  # Convert to h5ad and back h5ad_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".h5ad") writeH5AD(obj, h5ad_path, overwrite = TRUE, verbose = FALSE) obj_rt <- readH5AD(h5ad_path, verbose = FALSE) #> Warning: Layer 'data' is empty  cat("Original:", ncol(obj), "cells x", nrow(obj), "genes
") #> Original: 500 cells x 2000 genes cat("Round-trip:", ncol(obj_rt), "cells x", nrow(obj_rt), "genes
") #> Round-trip: 500 cells x 2000 genes cat("Reductions preserved:", paste(names(obj_rt@reductions), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Reductions preserved: pca, umap  DimPlot(obj_rt, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_annotations") +   ggplot2::ggtitle("After h5ad round-trip")

title: Format Mapping Reference
text: survives conversion across formats: Y = preserved, – = supported lost conversion.

title: Format Mapping Reference
text: Notes: Scale data stored h5ad (recompute ScaleData() sc.pp.scale()). Data (genes) written X; scale.data (variable features ) skipped.

title: Format Mapping Reference
text: mapping 1:1 since h5Seurat native format: Everything preserved exactly, including command logs tool results.

title: Format Mapping Reference
text: Seurat assay becomes separate modality: Modality name mapping: reverse mapping applies reading h5mu files.

title: Format Mapping Reference
text: Limitations: Variable features PCA standard deviations natively supported Loom. Feature metadata columns flattened row attributes.

title: Format Mapping Reference
text: Zarr format follows AnnData/h5ad layout uses Zarr storage: Zarr uses slot mapping h5ad. Direct streaming converters (H5ADToZarr(), ZarrToH5AD(), etc.) transfer data without constructing Seurat object memory.

title: Getting Started with scConvert
text: Install scConvert GitHub:
code: remotes::install_github("mianaz/scConvert")

title: Getting Started with scConvert
text: scConvert() function universal dispatcher: give source destination, picks fastest conversion path automatically. File formats detected extensions. scConvert() works supported format pair – h5ad, h5Seurat, Zarr, Loom, RDS, .
code: h5ad_file <- system.file("testdata", "pbmc_small.h5ad", package = "scConvert")  h5seurat_out <- file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc.h5seurat") scConvert(h5ad_file, dest = h5seurat_out, overwrite = TRUE)  zarr_out <- file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc.zarr") scConvert(h5ad_file, dest = zarr_out, overwrite = TRUE)

text: Starting Seurat object, write format collaborators downstream tools need.
code: h5ad_out <- file.path(tempdir(), "output.h5ad") writeH5AD(obj, h5ad_out, verbose = FALSE)  h5s_out <- file.path(tempdir(), "output.h5seurat") writeH5Seurat(obj, h5s_out, overwrite = TRUE, verbose = FALSE)  zarr_out2 <- file.path(tempdir(), "output.zarr") writeZarr(obj, zarr_out2, verbose = FALSE) sizes <- data.frame(   Format = c("h5ad", "h5Seurat", "Zarr"),   Size_MB = round(c(     file.size(h5ad_out),     file.size(h5s_out),     sum(file.info(list.files(zarr_out2, recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE))$size)   ) / 1024^2, 2) ) knitr::kable(sizes, col.names = c("Format", "Size (MB)"))

title: Getting Started with scConvert
text: use format: h5ad – sharing Python users submitting CELLxGENE. h5Seurat – archiving Seurat object selective loading support. Zarr – cloud-friendly, chunk-based access (e.g., S3 / GCS). Loom – interoperability loompy, velocyto, legacy pipelines. RDS – quick local save/load within R (HDF5 dependency).

title: Getting Started with scConvert
text: converting, quick sanity check confirms dimensions cell identifiers preserved.
code: original <- readH5AD(h5ad_file) converted <- readH5Seurat(h5seurat_out)  stopifnot(identical(dim(original), dim(converted))) stopifnot(identical(sort(colnames(original)), sort(colnames(converted))))  cat("Dimensions match:", paste(dim(original), collapse = " x "), "\n") #> Dimensions match: 2000 x 214 cat("Cell names match:", length(colnames(original)), "cells verified\n") #> Cell names match: 214 cells verified

title: Getting Started with scConvert
text: Convert Seurat AnnData – layer mapping, Python interop, round-trip verification. -Memory vs -Disk Conversion – hub path, streaming, C binary large datasets. Direct H5AD Loading – selective loading BPCells atlas-scale data. Multimodal H5MU – CITE-seq ATAC+RNA via MuData. Zarr Format – cloud-native storage streaming converters. Spatial Technologies – Visium, MERFISH, SpatialData support. CLI Usage – standalone C binary batch conversion without R.

title: Working with h5Seurat Files
text: scConvert ships 500-cell PBMC dataset h5Seurat format. readH5Seurat() loads directly fully functional Seurat object. loaded object contains nine annotated cell types PCA, UMAP, neighbor graphs:  Expression data fully available. LYZ monocyte marker:
code: h5seurat_file <- system.file("extdata", "pbmc_demo.h5seurat", package = "scConvert") pbmc <- readH5Seurat(h5seurat_file) pbmc #> An object of class Seurat  #> 2000 features across 500 samples within 1 assay  #> Active assay: RNA (2000 features, 2000 variable features) #>  2 layers present: counts, data #>  2 dimensional reductions calculated: pca, umap DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_annotations",         label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.5) + NoLegend() FeaturePlot(pbmc, features = "LYZ", pt.size = 0.5)

what: Write a Seurat object to h5Seurat
title: Working with h5Seurat Files
text: writeH5Seurat() saves Seurat object h5Seurat format, preserving assays, dimensional reductions, graphs, cell metadata.
code: pbmc_seurat <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "pbmc_demo.rds", package = "scConvert")) h5seurat_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".h5Seurat") writeH5Seurat(pbmc_seurat, filename = h5seurat_path, overwrite = TRUE) cat("File size:", round(file.size(h5seurat_path) / 1e6, 1), "MB\n") #> File size: 2.3 MB

title: Working with h5Seurat Files
text: Read written file back confirm UMAP coordinates, cluster labels, expression values identical original.
code: pbmc_rt <- readH5Seurat(h5seurat_path) library(patchwork) p1 <- DimPlot(pbmc_seurat, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_annotations",               label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.5) + NoLegend() + ggtitle("Original (.rds)") p2 <- DimPlot(pbmc_rt, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_annotations",               label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.5) + NoLegend() + ggtitle("Round-trip (.h5Seurat)") p1 + p2

title: Working with h5Seurat Files
text: large datasets, can load need. useful multi-gigabyte h5Seurat file want make quick UMAP plot. Load RNA assay UMAP, skipping PCA graphs: light object still supports visualization since UMAP coordinates present:
code: pbmc_light <- readH5Seurat(h5seurat_file, assays = "RNA",                            reductions = "umap", graphs = FALSE) pbmc_light #> An object of class Seurat  #> 2000 features across 500 samples within 1 assay  #> Active assay: RNA (2000 features, 2000 variable features) #>  2 layers present: counts, data #>  1 dimensional reduction calculated: umap DimPlot(pbmc_light, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_annotations",         label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.5) + NoLegend()

title: Working with h5Seurat Files
text: loading data, can inspect stored h5Seurat file using scConnect(). opens lightweight connection .index() summarizes contents – assays, reductions, graphs, images available. especially useful large files want decide load committing memory.
code: hfile <- scConnect(h5seurat_file) hfile$index() #> Data for assay RNA★ (default assay) #>    counts      data    scale.data #>      ✔          ✔          ✖      #> Dimensional reductions: #>         Embeddings  Loadings  Projected  JackStraw  #>  pca:       ✔          ✔          ✖          ✖      #>  umap:      ✔          ✖          ✖          ✖ hfile$close_all()

text: violin plot shows LYZ expression distribution across nine cell types:
code: VlnPlot(pbmc, features = "LYZ", group.by = "seurat_annotations", pt.size = 0) +   NoLegend()

title: Working with h5Seurat Files
code: unlink(h5seurat_path)

title: h5Seurat Format Specification
text: h5Seurat native HDF5 format Seurat objects. stores components – expression matrices, metadata, reductions, graphs, spatial data – single .h5Seurat file. vignette provides quick example followed format specification.

title: h5Seurat Format Specification
code:
obj <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "pbmc_demo.rds", package = "scConvert"))  h5s_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".h5Seurat") writeH5Seurat(obj, h5s_path, overwrite = TRUE, verbose = FALSE)  obj_loaded <- readH5Seurat(h5s_path, verbose = FALSE) #> Validating h5Seurat file #> Warning: Adding a command log without an assay associated with it #> Adding a command log without an assay associated with it cat("Cells:", ncol(obj_loaded), "| Genes:", nrow(obj_loaded), "
") #> Cells: 500 | Genes: 2000 cat("Assays:", paste(names(obj_loaded@assays), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Assays: RNA cat("Reductions:", paste(names(obj_loaded@reductions), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Reductions: pca, umap  DimPlot(obj_loaded, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_annotations") +   ggplot2::ggtitle("Loaded from h5Seurat")

title: h5Seurat Format Specification
text: Every h5Seurat file following layout: Required attributes root group:

title: h5Seurat Format Specification
text: assay stored assays/{name}/: Required attribute: key (string) – assay key prefix (e.g., "rna_"). Seurat v5 Assay5 objects automatically converted layout, layers stored separate matrices.

title: h5Seurat Format Specification
text: reduction stored reductions/{name}/: Required attributes: active.assay (string), key (string), global (logical).

title: h5Seurat Format Specification
text: graph stored graphs/{name}/ sparse matrix group: Required attribute: dims (2 integers) – matrix dimensions. Optional attribute: assay.used (string).

title: h5Seurat Format Specification
text: Stored CSC (compressed sparse column) three datasets: data, indices, indptr. matches dgCMatrix layout R. dims attribute records (n_rows, n_cols).

title: h5Seurat Format Specification
text: Stored group two datasets: levels – string dataset unique levels values – integer dataset 1-based level indices avoids HDF5 enum types, supported HDF5 implementations.

title: h5Seurat Format Specification
text: Stored either: Dataset – compound HDF5 dataset (factors present) Group – one dataset per column, factors sub-groups Group-based data frames may include colnames attribute column ordering row.names dataset.

title: h5Seurat Format Specification
text: Encoded integers: FALSE = 0, TRUE = 1, NA = 2. optional s3class = "logical" attribute enforces type read.

title: h5Seurat Format Specification
text: strings variable-length UTF-8 encoded, datasets attributes.

title: Immune Repertoire Metadata Preservation
text: Single-cell immune profiling experiments often pair gene expression TCR/BCR sequencing. Tools like scRepertoire add clonotype annotations – clonotype IDs, V/D/J gene usage, CDR3 sequences, clone frequencies – cell-level metadata. scConvert preserves metadata format conversions, TCR/BCR annotations survive roundtrips Seurat h5ad.

what: Load PBMC data and add synthetic TCR metadata
title: Immune Repertoire Metadata Preservation
text: start shipped PBMC demo (500 cells, 9 cell types) add synthetic TCR annotations demonstrate metadata preservation.
code:
pbmc_path <- system.file("extdata", "pbmc_demo.rds", package = "scConvert") obj <- readRDS(pbmc_path)  cat("Cells:", ncol(obj), "

previous_headings: Load PBMC data and add synthetic TCR metadata
title: Immune Repertoire Metadata Preservation
text: real workflow, columns come scRepertoire’s combineExpression(). create synthetic clonotype data illustrate conversion.
code: set.seed(42) n_cells <- ncol(obj)  # 8 clonotypes with power-law-like frequencies clonotype_ids <- paste0("clonotype_", sprintf("%03d", 1:8)) weights <- c(30, 22, 16, 12, 8, 5, 4, 3) assignments <- sample(seq_along(clonotype_ids), n_cells,                        replace = TRUE, prob = weights)  obj$clonotype_id <- factor(clonotype_ids[assignments], levels = clonotype_ids)  # V/J gene usage tra_genes <- c("TRAV1-2", "TRAV12-1", "TRAV38-2", "TRAV21",                "TRAV8-6", "TRAV26-1", "TRAV13-1", "TRAV29") trb_genes <- c("TRBV6-1", "TRBV28", "TRBV5-1", "TRBV7-2",                "TRBV19", "TRBV12-3", "TRBV4-1", "TRBV20-1") obj$tra_gene <- tra_genes[assignments] obj$trb_gene <- trb_genes[assignments]  # Clone frequency freq_tbl <- table(assignments) obj$clone_frequency <- as.integer(freq_tbl[as.character(assignments)])  cat("TCR columns added:", paste(c("clonotype_id", "tra_gene",     "trb_gene", "clone_frequency"), collapse = ", "), "\n") #> TCR columns added: clonotype_id, tra_gene, trb_gene, clone_frequency

title: CITE-seq Analysis Workflow
text: CITE-seq (Cellular Indexing Transcriptomes Epitopes Sequencing) measures RNA transcription surface protein abundance cells. produces two assays per cell: RNA gene expression ADT (antibody-derived tag) protein counts. h5mu format ideal storing modalities single file. scConvert handles assays format conversion, provides two export paths: h5ad: Exports single assay (RNA ADT) per file, scanpy workflows h5mu: Exports assays one file, muon/MuData workflows

title: CITE-seq Analysis Workflow
text: shipped citeseq_demo.h5mu contains 500 cells RNA (2,000 genes) ADT (10 antibodies: CD3, CD4, CD8, CD45RA, CD56, CD16, CD11c, CD14, CD19, CD34), pre-processed PCA, UMAP, clustering.
code:
h5mu_file <- system.file("extdata", "citeseq_demo.h5mu", package = "scConvert") obj <- readH5MU(h5mu_file)  cat("Cells:", ncol(obj), "
") #> Cells: 500 cat("Assays:", paste(names(obj@assays), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Assays: ADT, RNA cat("RNA features:", nrow(obj[["RNA"]]), "
") #> RNA features: 2000 cat("ADT features:", nrow(obj[["ADT"]]), "
") #> ADT features: 10 cat("ADT markers:", paste(rownames(obj[["ADT"]]), collapse = ", "), "
") #> ADT markers: CD3, CD4, CD8, CD45RA, CD56, CD16, CD11c, CD14, CD19, CD34

title: CITE-seq Analysis Workflow
code: DimPlot(obj, group.by = "seurat_clusters", label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.8) +   ggtitle("RNA-based clusters")

title: CITE-seq Analysis Workflow
text: ADT markers reveal cell-surface protein levels complement transcriptomic clusters. reading h5mu, ADT assay contains raw counts, normalize CLR plotting. CD3 marks T cells; CD14 marks monocytes.
code: DefaultAssay(obj) <- "ADT" obj <- NormalizeData(obj, normalization.method = "CLR", margin = 2, verbose = FALSE) library(patchwork)  p1 <- FeaturePlot(obj, features = "CD3", pt.size = 0.8) + ggtitle("CD3 (T cells)") p2 <- FeaturePlot(obj, features = "CD14", pt.size = 0.8) + ggtitle("CD14 (Monocytes)") p1 + p2

title: CITE-seq Analysis Workflow
code: VlnPlot(obj, features = "CD3", group.by = "seurat_clusters", pt.size = 0.1) +   ggtitle("CD3 protein across clusters") +   NoLegend()

title: CITE-seq Analysis Workflow
text: writeH5AD() writes one assay time. default writes active assay (RNA). right choice standard scanpy workflows.
code: h5ad_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "citeseq_rna.h5ad") writeH5AD(obj, h5ad_path, overwrite = TRUE) cat("Wrote RNA to:", basename(h5ad_path), "\n") #> Wrote RNA to: citeseq_rna.h5ad cat("File size:", round(file.size(h5ad_path) / 1024^2, 1), "MB\n") #> File size: 0.7 MB

title: CITE-seq Analysis Workflow
text: writeH5MU() writes every assay separate modality, keeping RNA ADT together single file.
code: h5mu_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "citeseq_roundtrip.h5mu") writeH5MU(obj, h5mu_path, overwrite = TRUE) cat("Wrote RNA + ADT to:", basename(h5mu_path), "\n") #> Wrote RNA + ADT to: citeseq_roundtrip.h5mu cat("File size:", round(file.size(h5mu_path) / 1024^2, 1), "MB\n") #> File size: 0.8 MB

title: CITE-seq Analysis Workflow
code: loaded_h5ad <- readH5AD(h5ad_path) cat("h5ad loaded:", ncol(loaded_h5ad), "cells x", nrow(loaded_h5ad), "features\n") #> h5ad loaded: 500 cells x 2000 features cat("Assays:", paste(names(loaded_h5ad@assays), collapse = ", "), "\n") #> Assays: RNA

title: CITE-seq Analysis Workflow
code:
loaded_h5mu <- readH5MU(h5mu_path) cat("h5mu loaded:", ncol(loaded_h5mu), "cells
") #> h5mu loaded: 500 cells cat("Assays:", paste(names(loaded_h5mu@assays), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Assays: ADT, RNA cat("RNA features:", nrow(loaded_h5mu[["RNA"]]), "
") #> RNA features: 2000 cat("ADT features:", nrow(loaded_h5mu[["ADT"]]), "
") #> ADT features: 10

title: CITE-seq Analysis Workflow
code: # Compare against the original h5mu-loaded object orig <- readH5MU(h5mu_file) common_cells <- intersect(colnames(orig), colnames(loaded_h5mu))  common_genes <- intersect(rownames(orig[["RNA"]]), rownames(loaded_h5mu[["RNA"]])) orig_rna <- as.numeric(GetAssayData(orig, assay = "RNA", layer = "counts")[   head(common_genes, 100), head(common_cells, 100)]) rt_rna <- as.numeric(GetAssayData(loaded_h5mu, assay = "RNA", layer = "counts")[   head(common_genes, 100), head(common_cells, 100)]) cat("RNA counts identical:", identical(orig_rna, rt_rna), "\n") #> RNA counts identical: TRUE  common_adt <- intersect(rownames(orig[["ADT"]]), rownames(loaded_h5mu[["ADT"]])) orig_adt <- as.numeric(GetAssayData(orig, assay = "ADT", layer = "counts")[   common_adt, head(common_cells, 100)]) rt_adt <- as.numeric(GetAssayData(loaded_h5mu, assay = "ADT", layer = "counts")[   common_adt, head(common_cells, 100)]) cat("ADT counts identical:", identical(orig_adt, rt_adt), "\n") #> ADT counts identical: TRUE

title: CITE-seq Analysis Workflow
text: exported files directly readable Python.
code: # Requires Python: pip install scanpy import scanpy as sc  adata = sc.read_h5ad("citeseq_rna.h5ad") print(adata) sc.pl.umap(adata, color="seurat_clusters") # Requires Python: pip install mudata import mudata as md  mdata = md.read_h5mu("citeseq.h5mu") print(mdata) for name, mod in mdata.mod.items():     print(f"  {name}: {mod.n_obs} cells x {mod.n_vars} features")

title: Multimodal Data with MuData (h5mu)
text: MuData format (.h5mu) stores multimodal single-cell data single file. modality (RNA, protein, ATAC) lives separate AnnData object, sharing common set cell barcodes. native format muon Python. scConvert reads writes h5mu natively – Python MuDataSeurat required.

title: Multimodal Data with MuData (h5mu)
text: shipped citeseq_demo.h5mu contains 500 CITE-seq cells two modalities: RNA (2,000 genes) ADT (10 surface protein antibodies). readH5MU() loads Seurat object assays intact.
code:
h5mu_file <- system.file("extdata", "citeseq_demo.h5mu", package = "scConvert") obj <- readH5MU(h5mu_file)  cat("Cells:", ncol(obj), "
") #> Cells: 500 cat("Assays:", paste(names(obj@assays), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Assays: ADT, RNA cat("RNA features:", nrow(obj[["RNA"]]), "
") #> RNA features: 2000 cat("ADT features:", nrow(obj[["ADT"]]), "
") #> ADT features: 10

title: Multimodal Data with MuData (h5mu)
code: DimPlot(obj, group.by = "seurat_clusters", label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.8) +   ggtitle("CITE-seq clusters (loaded from h5mu)")

title: Multimodal Data with MuData (h5mu)
text: reading h5mu, ADT assay contains raw counts. Normalize CLR (centered log-ratio) plotting protein markers.
code: DefaultAssay(obj) <- "ADT" obj <- NormalizeData(obj, normalization.method = "CLR", margin = 2, verbose = FALSE) FeaturePlot(obj, features = "CD3", pt.size = 0.8) +   ggtitle("CD3 protein (ADT) -- loaded from h5mu") DefaultAssay(obj) <- "RNA"

title: Multimodal Data with MuData (h5mu)
text: Load dataset Seurat .rds form, write h5mu, read back compare. demonstrates scConvert preserves assays full write/read cycle.

title: Multimodal Data with MuData (h5mu)
text: writeH5MU() automatically maps Seurat assay names standard MuData conventions: readH5MU() reverses mapping loading.
code: orig <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "citeseq_demo.rds", package = "scConvert"))  h5mu_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "citeseq_roundtrip.h5mu") writeH5MU(orig, h5mu_path, overwrite = TRUE) cat("Wrote:", round(file.size(h5mu_path) / 1024^2, 1), "MB\n") #> Wrote: 0.8 MB  loaded <- readH5MU(h5mu_path) cat("Loaded:", ncol(loaded), "cells,", paste(names(loaded@assays), collapse = ", "), "\n") #> Loaded: 500 cells, ADT, RNA

title: Multimodal Data with MuData (h5mu)
code: library(patchwork)  p1 <- DimPlot(orig, group.by = "seurat_clusters", label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.8) +   ggtitle("Original (.rds)") + NoLegend() p2 <- DimPlot(loaded, group.by = "seurat_clusters", label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.8) +   ggtitle("After h5mu roundtrip") + NoLegend() p1 + p2

title: Multimodal Data with MuData (h5mu)
code:
cat("Cell count match:", ncol(orig) == ncol(loaded), "
") #> Cell count match: TRUE cat("Assays match:", identical(sort(names(orig@assays)), sort(names(loaded@assays))), "
") #> Assays match: TRUE  common_cells <- intersect(colnames(orig), colnames(loaded)) common_genes <- intersect(rownames(orig[["RNA"]]), rownames(loaded[["RNA"]])) orig_rna <- as.numeric(GetAssayData(orig, assay = "RNA", layer = "counts")[   head(common_genes, 100), head(common_cells, 100)]) rt_rna <- as.numeric(GetAssayData(loaded, assay = "RNA", layer = "counts")[   head(common_genes, 100), head(common_cells, 100)]) cat("RNA counts identical:", identical(orig_rna, rt_rna), "
") #> RNA counts identical: TRUE  common_adt <- intersect(rownames(orig[["ADT"]]), rownames(loaded[["ADT"]])) orig_adt <- as.numeric(GetAssayData(orig, assay = "ADT", layer = "counts")[   common_adt, head(common_cells, 100)]) rt_adt <- as.numeric(GetAssayData(loaded, assay = "ADT", layer = "counts")[   common_adt, head(common_cells, 100)]) cat("ADT counts identical:", identical(orig_adt, rt_adt), "
") #> ADT counts identical: TRUE

title: Multimodal Data with MuData (h5mu)

code: custom <- readH5MU(h5mu_file, assay.names = c(rna = "RNA", prot = "Protein")) cat("Assays with custom mapping:", paste(names(custom@assays), collapse = ", "), "\n") #> Assays with custom mapping: Protein, RNA

title: scATAC-seq Data Conversion
text: Single-cell ATAC-seq measures chromatin accessibility single-cell resolution. core data structure peak--cell count matrix – structurally identical gene--cell RNA matrix. scConvert converts matrix along cell metadata, embeddings, cluster labels formats, enabling interoperability R (Seurat/Signac) Python (scanpy/episcanpy) workflows. vignette demonstrates h5ad roundtrip using shipped PBMC demo data stand-. matrix structure conversion mechanics regardless whether rows represent genes peaks.

title: scATAC-seq Data Conversion
code:
obj <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "pbmc_demo.rds", package = "scConvert"))  cat("Cells:", ncol(obj), "
") #> Cells: 500 cat("Features:", nrow(obj), "
") #> Features: 2000 cat("Reductions:", paste(names(obj@reductions), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Reductions: pca, umap cat("Metadata columns:", paste(colnames(obj[[]]), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Metadata columns: orig.ident, nCount_RNA, nFeature_RNA, seurat_annotations, percent.mt, RNA_snn_res.0.5, seurat_clusters DimPlot(obj, group.by = "seurat_annotations", label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.8) +   ggtitle("PBMC clusters (original)")

title: scATAC-seq Data Conversion
text: Write Seurat object h5ad read back.
code:
h5ad_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc_atac_demo.h5ad")  writeH5AD(obj, h5ad_path, overwrite = TRUE) cat("Wrote:", basename(h5ad_path), "
") #> Wrote: pbmc_atac_demo.h5ad cat("File size:", round(file.size(h5ad_path) / 1024^2, 1), "MB
") #> File size: 0.9 MB  loaded <- readH5AD(h5ad_path) cat("Loaded:", ncol(loaded), "cells x", nrow(loaded), "features
") #> Loaded: 500 cells x 2000 features

title: scATAC-seq Data Conversion
code:
cat("Cells match:", ncol(obj) == ncol(loaded), "
") #> Cells match: TRUE cat("Features match:", nrow(obj) == nrow(loaded), "
") #> Features match: TRUE  orig_cells <- sort(colnames(obj)) rt_cells <- sort(colnames(loaded)) cat("Barcodes identical:", identical(orig_cells, rt_cells), "
") #> Barcodes identical: TRUE  orig_features <- sort(rownames(obj)) rt_features <- sort(rownames(loaded)) cat("Feature names identical:", identical(orig_features, rt_features), "
") #> Feature names identical: TRUE

title: scATAC-seq Data Conversion
code: common_cells <- intersect(colnames(obj), colnames(loaded)) common_feats <- intersect(rownames(obj), rownames(loaded))  orig_vals <- as.numeric(GetAssayData(obj, layer = "counts")[   head(common_feats, 200), head(common_cells, 200)]) rt_vals <- as.numeric(GetAssayData(loaded, layer = "counts")[   head(common_feats, 200), head(common_cells, 200)]) cat("Count values identical:", identical(orig_vals, rt_vals), "\n") #> Count values identical: TRUE

title: scATAC-seq Data Conversion
code:
shared_cols <- intersect(colnames(obj[[]]), colnames(loaded[[]])) cat("Metadata columns preserved:", length(shared_cols), "/", ncol(obj[[]]), "
") #> Metadata columns preserved: 7 / 7 cat("Columns:", paste(shared_cols, collapse = ", "), "
") #> Columns: orig.ident, nCount_RNA, nFeature_RNA, seurat_annotations, percent.mt, RNA_snn_res.0.5, seurat_clusters  if ("seurat_annotations" %in% shared_cols) {   orig_ann <- as.character(obj$seurat_annotations[common_cells])   rt_ann <- as.character(loaded$seurat_annotations[common_cells])   cat("Cell annotations match:", identical(orig_ann, rt_ann), "
") } #> Cell annotations match: TRUE

title: scATAC-seq Data Conversion
code:
cat("Original reductions:", paste(names(obj@reductions), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Original reductions: pca, umap cat("Loaded reductions:", paste(names(loaded@reductions), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Loaded reductions: pca, umap  if ("umap" %in% names(loaded@reductions)) {   orig_umap <- Embeddings(obj, "umap")[common_cells, ]   rt_umap <- Embeddings(loaded, "umap")[common_cells, ]   max_diff <- max(abs(orig_umap - rt_umap))   cat("UMAP max absolute difference:", max_diff, "
") } #> UMAP max absolute difference: 0

what: What is preserved vs. what needs separate handling
title: scATAC-seq Data Conversion
text: real scATAC-seq data, scConvert preserves core data Signac-specific components require separate handling:

what: Upgrading to ChromatinAssay (if Signac is available)
title: scATAC-seq Data Conversion
text: loading h5ad file containing peak data, can upgrade standard Seurat assay Signac ChromatinAssay peak-aware analyses.
code: library(Signac)  # Load h5ad containing peak-by-cell matrix atac <- readH5AD("peaks.h5ad")  # Upgrade to ChromatinAssay peak_counts <- GetAssayData(atac, layer = "counts") atac[["peaks"]] <- CreateChromatinAssay(   counts = peak_counts,   sep = c("-", "-"),   min.cells = 0,   min.features = 0 )  # Re-attach annotations if needed # library(EnsDb.Hsapiens.v86) # Annotation(atac) <- GetGRangesFromEnsDb(ensdb = EnsDb.Hsapiens.v86) # Fragments(atac) <- CreateFragmentObject("fragments.tsv.gz")

title: scATAC-seq Data Conversion
text: exported h5ad directly readable scanpy episcanpy.
code: # Requires Python: pip install scanpy import scanpy as sc  adata = sc.read_h5ad("peaks.h5ad") print(adata) print(f"Peak names: {list(adata.var_names[:5])}")  # Standard scanpy pipeline works on peak matrices sc.pp.normalize_total(adata) sc.pp.log1p(adata) sc.pp.pca(adata) sc.pp.neighbors(adata) sc.tl.umap(adata) sc.pl.umap(adata, color="seurat_clusters")

code: unlink(h5ad_path)

title: Spatial Technologies Overview
text: Spatial transcriptomics spans range technologies – spot-based platforms like Visium Slide-seq subcellular-resolution methods like Xenium MERFISH. scConvert handles spatial data platform produces Seurat object h5ad file, preserving coordinates, images, metadata format conversion.

title: Spatial Technologies Overview
text: technologies store spatial coordinates h5ad obsm/spatial field. Visium additionally stores tissue images scale factors uns/spatial.

title: Spatial Technologies Overview
text: demonstrate full roundtrip shipped Visium demo dataset (400 mouse brain spots, 1,500 genes, 15 clusters).
code:
spatial_path <- system.file("extdata", "spatial_demo.rds", package = "scConvert") brain <- readRDS(spatial_path)  cat("Spots:", ncol(brain), "
") #> Spots: 400 cat("Genes:", nrow(brain), "
") #> Genes: 1500 cat("Image:", paste(names(brain@images), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Image: anterior1 cat("Assay:", paste(names(brain@assays), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Assay: Spatial

title: Spatial Technologies Overview
text: Ttr (Transthyretin) expressed choroid plexus shows strong spatial localization.
code: SpatialFeaturePlot(brain, features = "Ttr", pt.size.factor = 1.6) +   ggplot2::ggtitle("Ttr expression (original)")

title: Spatial Technologies Overview
code:
h5ad_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".h5ad") writeH5AD(brain, h5ad_file, overwrite = TRUE) brain_rt <- readH5AD(h5ad_file, verbose = FALSE)  cat("Roundtrip spots:", ncol(brain_rt), "
") #> Roundtrip spots: 400 cat("Roundtrip genes:", nrow(brain_rt), "
") #> Roundtrip genes: 1500 cat("Image preserved:", length(brain_rt@images) > 0, "
") #> Image preserved: TRUE

title: Spatial Technologies Overview
code:
cat("Barcodes match:", all(colnames(brain) == colnames(brain_rt)), "
") #> Barcodes match: TRUE cat("Features match:", all(rownames(brain) == rownames(brain_rt)), "
") #> Features match: TRUE cat("Clusters match:",     all(as.character(brain$seurat_clusters) ==         as.character(brain_rt$seurat_clusters)), "
") #> Clusters match: TRUE

title: Spatial Technologies Overview
code: DimPlot(brain_rt, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_clusters",         label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggplot2::ggtitle("UMAP clusters (after h5ad roundtrip)")

title: Spatial Technologies Overview
text: Nrgn (Neurogranin) cortical neuron marker different spatial pattern Ttr.
code: # Round-tripped objects may have only the counts layer populated (scConvert # writes X from counts when no normalised data is present). Ensure a data # layer exists before plotting. readH5AD() names the default assay "RNA" # unless assay.name is passed, so look up the actual default rather than # assuming the input's assay name survives the roundtrip. rt_assay <- SeuratObject::DefaultAssay(brain_rt) if (!"data" %in% SeuratObject::Layers(brain_rt[[rt_assay]]) ||     length(SeuratObject::GetAssayData(brain_rt, layer = "data")) == 0L) {   brain_rt <- Seurat::NormalizeData(brain_rt, verbose = FALSE) } FeaturePlot(brain_rt, features = "Nrgn", reduction = "umap") +   ggplot2::ggtitle("Nrgn expression (UMAP, after roundtrip)")

title: Spatial Technologies Overview
text: h5ad file spatial coordinates, workflow applies: Technologies without tissue images (Slide-seq, MERFISH, CODEX) store coordinates obsm/spatial. scConvert reads Seurat object makes available plotting downstream analysis.
code: # Load spatial h5ad from any platform obj <- readH5AD("merfish_data.h5ad")  # Check what was detected cat("Images:", paste(names(obj@images), collapse = ", "), "\n")  # Convert to another format scConvert("merfish_data.h5ad", "merfish_data.h5seurat")

title: Spatial Technologies Overview
code: # Requires Python with scanpy installed import scanpy as sc  adata = sc.read_h5ad("brain.h5ad") print(adata)  # Works with any spatial technology sc.pl.spatial(adata, color="Ttr")  # Visium (with image) sc.pl.embedding(adata, basis="spatial", color="cluster")  # Generic spatial

title: Visium Spatial Data Conversion
text: 10x Genomics Visium captures spatially-resolved gene expression across tissue sections. converting Visium data Seurat h5ad, scConvert preserves spot coordinates, tissue images, scale factors, associated metadata data immediately usable R (Seurat) Python (scanpy/squidpy) ecosystems.

title: Visium Spatial Data Conversion
text: shipped spatial_demo.h5ad contains 400 mouse brain Visium spots 1,500 genes, PCA/UMAP reductions, cluster labels. readH5AD() rebuilds full Seurat spatial object, including tissue image.
code:
h5ad_file <- system.file("extdata", "spatial_demo.h5ad", package = "scConvert") brain <- readH5AD(h5ad_file, verbose = FALSE)  cat("Spots:", ncol(brain), "
") #> Spots: 400 cat("Genes:", nrow(brain), "
") #> Genes: 1500 cat("Image:", paste(names(brain@images), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Image: anterior1 cat("Assay:", paste(names(brain@assays), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Assay: RNA

what: Spatial expression of a brain marker
title: Visium Spatial Data Conversion
text: Hpca (Hippocalcin) marks hippocampal neurons shows clear regional expression mouse brain.
code: SpatialFeaturePlot(brain, features = "Hpca", pt.size.factor = 1.6) +   ggtitle("Hpca expression (loaded from h5ad)")

title: Visium Spatial Data Conversion
code: DimPlot(brain, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_clusters",         label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("UMAP clusters (loaded from h5ad)")

title: Visium Spatial Data Conversion
text: Write spatial object new h5ad file, read back, confirm coordinates, images, scale factors, expression values preserved.
code:
h5ad_rt <- file.path(tempdir(), "spatial_roundtrip.h5ad") writeH5AD(brain, h5ad_rt, overwrite = TRUE) cat("Wrote:", round(file.size(h5ad_rt) / 1024^2, 1), "MB
") #> Wrote: 3.2 MB  brain_rt <- readH5AD(h5ad_rt, verbose = FALSE) cat("Roundtrip spots:", ncol(brain_rt), "
") #> Roundtrip spots: 400 cat("Roundtrip genes:", nrow(brain_rt), "
") #> Roundtrip genes: 1500 cat("Image:", paste(names(brain_rt@images), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Image: anterior1

title: Visium Spatial Data Conversion
text: spatial expression pattern identical roundtrip.
code: SpatialFeaturePlot(brain_rt, features = "Hpca", pt.size.factor = 1.6) +   ggtitle("Hpca expression (after h5ad roundtrip)")

title: Visium Spatial Data Conversion
code:
cat("Dimensions match:",     ncol(brain) == ncol(brain_rt) && nrow(brain) == nrow(brain_rt), "
") #> Dimensions match: TRUE cat("Barcodes match:", all(colnames(brain) == colnames(brain_rt)), "
") #> Barcodes match: TRUE cat("Clusters match:",     all(as.character(brain$seurat_clusters) ==         as.character(brain_rt$seurat_clusters)), "
") #> Clusters match: TRUE

title: Visium Spatial Data Conversion
text: scConvert maintains full fidelity Visium spatial data conversion:

title: Visium Spatial Data Conversion
text: exported h5ad works directly scanpy squidpy. Images, coordinates, scale factors use standard scanpy conventions.
code: # Requires Python with scanpy and squidpy installed import scanpy as sc  adata = sc.read_h5ad("spatial_demo.h5ad") print(adata) print(f"Spatial coords shape: {adata.obsm['spatial'].shape}")  # Spatial scatter plot sc.pl.spatial(adata, color="seurat_clusters")  # Squidpy spatial analysis import squidpy as sq sq.gr.spatial_neighbors(adata, coord_type="generic") sq.gr.spatial_autocorr(adata, mode="moran", genes=["Hpca", "Ttr"])
