what: Universal Single-Cell Format Conversion for R
title: Universal Single-Cell Format Conversion for R
text: Universal single-cell format conversion R. Python required. scConvert converts 9 single-cell data formats hub architecture using Seurat universal intermediate, providing 50+ conversion paths single scConvert() interface.

title: Universal Single-Cell Format Conversion for R
code: # Install from GitHub devtools::install_github("mianaz/scConvert")

title: Universal Single-Cell Format Conversion for R
code: library(scConvert)  # One-line conversion between any format pair scConvert("data.h5ad", dest = "h5seurat") scConvert("data.h5mu", dest = "rds") scConvert(seurat_obj, dest = "output.h5ad")  # Direct h5ad loading with full metadata preservation obj <- readH5AD("data.h5ad")  # Memory-efficient atlas-scale loading via BPCells obj <- readH5AD("atlas_1M_cells.h5ad", use.bpcells = TRUE)  # Multimodal (CITE-seq, ATAC+RNA) support obj <- readH5MU("citeseq.h5mu")   # auto-maps rna->RNA, prot->ADT writeH5MU(obj, "output.h5mu")  # TileDB-SOMA (CELLxGENE Census) obj <- readSOMA("soma://collection/measurement") writeSOMA(obj, "output.soma")  # SpatialData zarr stores obj <- readSpatialData("experiment.spatialdata.zarr") writeSpatialData(obj, "output.spatialdata.zarr")

title: Universal Single-Cell Format Conversion for R
text: Three conversion tiers provide optimal speed format pair: C CLI: HDF5 pairs (h5ad, h5Seurat, h5mu, Loom) – streaming, constant memory R streaming: Direct format--format without materializing Seurat object Hub path: Load → Seurat → Save RDS, SCE, cross-tier pairs
code: ┌─── h5ad ───┐                   │      ╎     │                   ├─── h5Seurat│                   │      ╎     ├── C CLI (fastest, streaming)    ┌────────┐     ├─── h5mu ───┤    │ Seurat │─────┤      ╎     │    └────────┘     ├─── Loom ───┘                   │                   ├─── Zarr ········ R streaming (no Seurat)                   │                   ├─── TileDB-SOMA                   │                   ├─── SpatialData                   │                   ├─── RDS                   │                   └─── SCE (Bioconductor)     ─── hub path (via Seurat)    ╎╎╎ direct HDF5 streaming (no intermediate)    ··· R streaming via temp h5seurat

title: Universal Single-Cell Format Conversion for R
text: Synthetic sparse h5ad (20K genes, 5% density), median 3 runs: C writer writes h5ad directly via .Call(), using zero-copy CSC↔︎CSR reinterpretation (sparse transpose needed). Use gzip = 0 local/temporary files (6× faster), gzip = 1 good speed/size balance (252 MB vs 1.2 GB 100K cells). BPCells -disk loading (use.bpcells) reduces memory ~87% atlas scale. Benchmarked Apple M4 Max, 128 GB RAM.

title: Universal Single-Cell Format Conversion for R
text: standalone C binary HDF5-based conversions (h5ad, h5Seurat, h5mu, Loom) without R Python: Options: --assay <name>, --gzip <0-9>, --overwrite, --quiet, --version, --help
code: # Build (requires HDF5 headers) cd src && make -f Makefile.cli  # Optional: install into the R package tree so `library(scConvert)` picks it up make -f Makefile.cli install-bin   # copies to ../inst/bin/scconvert  # Convert between any HDF5 format pair ./scconvert data.h5ad data.h5seurat ./scconvert data.h5seurat data.h5ad --assay RNA ./scconvert multimodal.h5mu multimodal.h5seurat ./scconvert data.h5ad data.loom ./scconvert data.loom data.h5seurat

what: Convert a logical to an integer — BoolToInt
title: Convert a logical to an integer — BoolToInt
text: Unlike programming languages, R three possible logical (boolean) values: TRUE, FALSE, NA; moreover, NA value representations data types, NA_integer_, NA_real_, NA_character_. Simply writing logical values HDF5 file cause issues trying read data another language, Python. encode three logical values languages, can encode logicals integers: FALSE becomes 0L TRUE becomes 1L NA becomes 2L encoding scheme allows languages handle NAs manner preserving three logicals R

code: # \donttest{ # Characters can either be variable-width UTF8-encoded or # fixed-width ASCII-encoded scConvert:::GuessDType(x = 'hello') #> Class: H5T_STRING #> Datatype: H5T_STRING { #>       STRSIZE H5T_VARIABLE; #>       STRPAD H5T_STR_NULLTERM; #>       CSET H5T_CSET_UTF8; #>       CTYPE H5T_C_S1; #>    } scConvert:::GuessDType(x = 'hello', stype = 'ascii7') #> Class: H5T_STRING #> Datatype: H5T_STRING { #>       STRSIZE 7; #>       STRPAD H5T_STR_NULLTERM; #>       CSET H5T_CSET_ASCII; #>       CTYPE H5T_C_S1; #>    }  # Data frames are a compound type; character columns follow the same rules # as character vectors df <- data.frame(x = c('g1', 'g2', 'g3'), y = 1, 2, 3, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) scConvert:::GuessDType(x = df) #> Class: H5T_COMPOUND #> Datatype: H5T_COMPOUND { #>       H5T_STRING { #>          STRSIZE H5T_VARIABLE; #>          STRPAD H5T_STR_NULLTERM; #>          CSET H5T_CSET_UTF8; #>          CTYPE H5T_C_S1; #>       } "x" : 0; #>       H5T_IEEE_F64LE "y" : 8; #>       H5T_IEEE_F64LE "X2" : 16; #>       H5T_IEEE_F64LE "X3" : 24; #>    } scConvert:::GuessDType(x = df, stype = 'ascii7') #> Class: H5T_COMPOUND #> Datatype: H5T_COMPOUND { #>       H5T_STRING { #>          STRSIZE 7; #>          STRPAD H5T_STR_NULLTERM; #>          CSET H5T_CSET_ASCII; #>          CTYPE H5T_C_S1; #>       } "x" : 0; #>       H5T_IEEE_F64LE "y" : 7; #>       H5T_IEEE_F64LE "X2" : 15; #>       H5T_IEEE_F64LE "X3" : 23; #>    }  # Logicals are turned into integers to ensure compatibility with Python # TRUE evaluates to 1, FALSE to 0, and NA to 2 scConvert:::GuessDType(x = c(TRUE, FALSE, NA)) #> Class: H5T_INTEGER #> Datatype: H5T_STD_I32LE # }

title: Create and work with timestamps — Timestamp
text: 24-hour R-friendly format; stores date/time information following: Four-digit year (eg. “2020” year 2020) Two-digit month (eg. “04” month April) Two-digit date (eg. “02” second day month) letter “T” 24-hour two-digit time (eg. “15” 3:00 PM) Two-digit minute (eg “05” five minutes past hour) Two-digit second (eg. “05” five seconds minute) letter “Z” results timestamp format “YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS.SSSSSSZ” (eg.  “20200402T150505Z” April 4th, 2020 3:05:05 PM). Note:  considered “R-friendly” use precise  values seconds

title: Create and work with timestamps — Timestamp
text: standard format loom timestamps; stores date/time information  following: Four-digit year (eg. “2020” year 2020) Two-digit month (eg. “04” month April) Two-digit date (eg. “02” second day month) letter “T” 24-hour two-digit time (eg. “15” 3:00 PM) Two-digit minute (eg “05” five minutes past hour) Seconds precise millionth (six digits decimal) letter “Z” results timestamp format “YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS.SSSSSSZ” (eg.  “20200402T150505Z” April 4th, 2020 3:05:05 PM). Note:  considered “R-friendly” contains precise  values seconds. properly format time pretty-printing, please  remember strip precise seconds

title: Write data to an HDF5 group — WriteH5Group
text: Invisibly returns NULL

title: Write data to an HDF5 group — WriteH5Group
code: # \donttest{ # Setup an HDF5 file hfile <- hdf5r::H5File$new(filename = tempfile(fileext = '.h5'), mode = 'a') # }  # \donttest{ # Data frames are stored as either datasets or groups, depending on the # presence of factor columns df <- data.frame(   x = c('g1', 'g1', 'g2', 'g1', 'g2'),   y = 1:5,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # When no factor columns are present, the data frame is written as a single # HDF5 compound dataset WriteH5Group(x = df, name = 'df', hgroup = hfile) hfile[['df']] #> Class: H5Group #> Filename: /tmp/RtmphiabGh/file374945b5d2af.h5 #> Group: /df #> Attributes: colnames, _index #> Listing: #>    name    obj_type dataset.dims dataset.type_class #>  _index H5I_DATASET            5         H5T_STRING #>       x H5I_DATASET            5         H5T_STRING #>       y H5I_DATASET            5        H5T_INTEGER  # When factors are present, the data frame is written as a group # This is because h5py does not implement HDF5 Enums, so factor level # information would be lost df$x <- factor(x = df$x) WriteH5Group(x = df, name = 'df.factor', hgroup = hfile) hfile[['df.factor']] #> Class: H5Group #> Filename: /tmp/RtmphiabGh/file374945b5d2af.h5 #> Group: /df.factor #> Attributes: colnames, _index #> Listing: #>    name    obj_type dataset.dims dataset.type_class #>  _index H5I_DATASET            5         H5T_STRING #>       x   H5I_GROUP         <NA>               <NA> #>       y H5I_DATASET            5        H5T_INTEGER # }  # \donttest{ # Factors turn into a group with two components: values and levels # This is to preserve level information for HDF5 APIs that don't implement # the HDF5 Enum type (eg. h5py) # values corresponds to the integer values of each member of a factor # levels is a string dataset with one entry per level fctr <- factor(x = c('g1', 'g1', 'g2', 'g1', 'g2')) WriteH5Group(x = fctr, name = 'factor', hgroup = hfile) hfile[['factor']] #> Class: H5Group #> Filename: /tmp/RtmphiabGh/file374945b5d2af.h5 #> Group: /factor #> Listing: #>    name    obj_type dataset.dims dataset.type_class #>  levels H5I_DATASET            2         H5T_STRING #>  values H5I_DATASET            5        H5T_INTEGER # }  # \donttest{ # Logicals get encoded as integers with the following mapping # FALSE becomes 0L # TRUE becomes 1L # NA becomes 2L # These are stored as H5T_INTEGERS instead of H5T_LOGICALS # Additionally, an attribute called "s3class" is written with the value of "logical" WriteH5Group(c(TRUE, FALSE, NA), name = "logicals", hgroup = hfile) hfile[["logicals"]] #> Class: H5D #> Dataset: /logicals #> Filename: /tmp/RtmphiabGh/file374945b5d2af.h5 #> Access type: H5F_ACC_RDWR #> Attributes: s3class #> Datatype: H5T_STD_I32LE #> Space: Type=Simple     Dims=3     Maxdims=Inf #> Chunk: 2048 hfile[["logicals"]]$attr_open("s3class")$read() #> [1] "logical" # }  # \donttest{ # Close and remove the HDF5 file hfile$close_all() file.remove(hfile$filename) #> [1] TRUE # }

Use c("X") matrix-(fastest), subset c("X", "obs", "var", "obsm", "obsp", "varp", "layers", "uns"). file path stored misc[[".__h5ad_path__"]] deferred loading via scLoadMeta. use.c Use compiled C reader available (default: TRUE). title: Load a Seurat object from an h5Seurat file — readH5Seurat
code: readH5Seurat(file, ...)  # S3 method for class 'character' readH5Seurat(   file,   assays = NULL,   reductions = NULL,   graphs = NULL,   neighbors = NULL,   images = NULL,   meta.data = TRUE,   commands = TRUE,   misc = is.null(x = assays),   tools = is.null(x = assays),   verbose = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'H5File' readH5Seurat(   file,   assays = NULL,   reductions = NULL,   graphs = NULL,   neighbors = NULL,   images = NULL,   meta.data = TRUE,   commands = TRUE,   misc = is.null(x = assays),   tools = is.null(x = assays),   verbose = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'h5Seurat' readH5Seurat(   file,   assays = NULL,   reductions = NULL,   graphs = NULL,   neighbors = NULL,   images = NULL,   meta.data = TRUE,   commands = TRUE,   misc = is.null(x = assays),   tools = is.null(x = assays),   verbose = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'h5Seurat' as.Seurat(   x,   assays = NULL,   reductions = NULL,   graphs = NULL,   neighbors = NULL,   images = NULL,   meta.data = TRUE,   commands = TRUE,   misc = TRUE,   tools = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Load a Seurat object from an h5Seurat file — readH5Seurat
text: file, x Name h5Seurat file path (character) connected h5Seurat file load ... Arguments passed methods assays One : NULL (default): Load assays character vector names assays load (e.g., c("RNA", "ADT")) character vector specifying data layers load assays:    c("counts", "data") loads counts data layers, skipping scale.data named list fine-grained control, e.g., list(RNA = "data", ADT = c("data", "scale.data")) reductions One : NULL (default): Load reductions (PCA, UMAP, etc.) character vector names specific reductions (e.g., c("pca", "umap")) NA: Load global (assay-independent) reductions FALSE: Skip loading reductions Note: reductions associated loaded assay marked global loaded. graphs One : NULL (default): Load graphs character vector specific graph names (e.g., c("RNA_snn", "ADT_snn")) FALSE: Skip loading graphs Note: graphs associated loaded assays available. neighbors One : NULL (default): Load neighbor information character vector neighbor names FALSE: Skip neighbors images One : NULL (default): Load images (spatial experiments) character vector image names NA: Load global images FALSE: Skip images meta.data Logical; TRUE (default), load cell metadata commands Logical; TRUE (default), load command history. Commands loaded associated assays loaded. misc Logical; TRUE (default assays loaded), load miscellaneous data tools Logical; TRUE (default assays loaded), load tool-specific information verbose Logical; TRUE (default), show progress messages

title: Load a Seurat object from an h5Seurat file — readH5Seurat
text: Seurat object containing requested components

title: Load a Seurat object from an h5Seurat file — readH5Seurat
text: h5Seurat format highly flexible selective loading. particularly useful : Working large datasets loading everything exceed memory need specific assays reductions downstream analysis want quickly inspect object structure without full data loading

title: Load a Seurat object from an h5Seurat file — readH5Seurat
text: Seurat V5 objects multiple layers, can selectively load layers per assay. example, use assays = list(RNA = "data") load normalized expression layer, skipping raw counts scaled data.

title: Load a Seurat object from an h5Seurat file — readH5Seurat
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(scConvert)  # Load entire h5Seurat file seurat_obj <- readH5Seurat("data.h5seurat")  # Load only specific assays seurat_obj <- readH5Seurat("data.h5seurat", assays = c("RNA", "ADT"))  # Load only specific data layers (memory-efficient for large files) seurat_obj <- readH5Seurat("data.h5seurat", assays = c("data"))  # Only normalized expression  # Load specific assays with different layers seurat_obj <- readH5Seurat(   "data.h5seurat",   assays = list(RNA = c("data", "scale.data"), ADT = "data") )  # Load without reductions (faster) seurat_obj <- readH5Seurat("data.h5seurat", reductions = FALSE)  # Load UMAP and PCA reductions only seurat_obj <- readH5Seurat("data.h5seurat", reductions = c("umap", "pca"))  # Load spatial data without graphs (for Visium experiments) seurat_obj <- readH5Seurat("visium.h5seurat", images = TRUE, graphs = FALSE) } # }

code: readLoom(   file,   assay = NULL,   cells = "CellID",   features = "Gene",   normalized = NULL,   scaled = NULL,   filter = c("cells", "features", "all", "none"),   verbose = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'character' readLoom(file, ...)  # S3 method for class 'H5File' readLoom(file, ...)  # S3 method for class 'loom' readLoom(file, ...)  # S3 method for class 'loom' as.Seurat(   x,   assay = NULL,   cells = "CellID",   features = "Gene",   normalized = NULL,   scaled = NULL,   filter = c("cells", "features", "all", "none"),   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Load a Loom file as a Seurat object — readLoom
text: file, x Name Loom file (character path) loom object connection load data assay Name assay store expression data . NULL, search HDF5 attribute named SEURAT_ASSAY dataset /attrs/SEURAT_ASSAY assay name. Defaults "RNA" found. cells Name dataset /col_attrs containing cell/sample names. found, uses column indices cell names. features Name dataset /row_attrs containing feature/gene names. found, uses row indices feature names. normalized Name layer /layers load normalized expression data; special value "/matrix" loads main matrix normalized data instead counts scaled Name layer /layers load scaled data filter Logical; TRUE, keep cells features marked valid using /col_attrs/Valid /row_attrs/Valid attributes verbose Logical; TRUE (default), show progress updates ... Arguments passed methods

title: Load a Loom file as a Seurat object — readLoom
text: Seurat object containing expression matrix metadata Loom file

title: Load a Loom file as a Seurat object — readLoom
text: Loom format stores data specific HDF5 structure: /matrix: Main expression matrix (features x cells) /row_attrs: Feature-level metadata (gene names, coordinates, etc.) /col_attrs: Cell/sample-level metadata /layers: Additional expression layers (normalized, scaled, etc.)

title: Load a Loom file as a Seurat object — readLoom
text: Loading data loom files less version 3.0.0 currently supported

title: Load a Loom file as a Seurat object — readLoom
text: loom files version 3.0.0 higher, LoadLoom3.0 provides comprehensive loading support filtering, dimensional reductions, cell graphs.

title: Load a Loom file as a Seurat object — readLoom
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(scConvert)  # Load a basic Loom file seurat_obj <- readLoom("data.loom")  # Load with specific assay name seurat_obj <- readLoom("data.loom", assay = "RNA")  # Load and filter to valid cells/features only seurat_obj <- readLoom("data.loom", filter = TRUE)  # Load specific layers seurat_obj <- readLoom(   "data.loom",   normalized = "normalized",   scaled = "scaled" ) } # }

code: readSOMA(   uri,   measurement = "RNA",   obs_query = NULL,   var_query = NULL,   obs_column_names = NULL,   var_column_names = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Read a TileDB-SOMA experiment as a Seurat object — readSOMA
text: uri URI SOMA experiment. Can local path cloud URI (e.g., tiledb://... s3://...). measurement Name measurement use default assay (default: "RNA"). obs_query Optional value filter string cells, passed SOMADataFrame$read(value_filter = ...). example, "cell_type == 'T cell'". var_query Optional value filter string features. obs_column_names Optional character vector obs column names read. NULL (default), columns read. var_column_names Optional character vector var column names read. NULL (default), columns read. verbose Show progress messages

title: Read a TileDB-SOMA experiment as a Seurat object — readSOMA
text: Seurat object

title: Read a TileDB-SOMA experiment as a Seurat object — readSOMA
text: TileDB-SOMA cloud-native format underlying CELLxGENE Census (61M+ cells). function requires tiledbsoma R package. reader performs following steps: Opens SOMA experiment uri Reads obs (cell metadata) optional filtering Reads var (feature metadata) specified measurement Reads X matrix (data layer) sparse matrix Reads obsm entries dimensional reductions Reads obsp entries neighbor graphs Reads additional measurements extra Seurat assays Cell names determined following columns priority order: obs_id, _index, index, barcode, cell_id. none present, synthetic names (cell_1, cell_2, ...) generated.

title: Read a TileDB-SOMA experiment as a Seurat object — readSOMA
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Read a local SOMA experiment obj <- readSOMA("path/to/experiment")  # Read from CELLxGENE Census with filtering obj <- readSOMA(   uri = "s3://cellxgene-census/soma/...",   obs_query = "cell_type == 'T cell' & tissue == 'blood'" )  # Read a specific measurement obj <- readSOMA("path/to/experiment", measurement = "ATAC") } # }

what: scConvert: Universal Single-Cell Format Conversion for R — scConvert-package
title: scConvert: Universal Single-Cell Format Conversion for R — scConvert-package
text: Universal single-cell format conversion R. Converts h5ad (AnnData), h5Seurat, h5mu (MuData), Loom, Zarr, RDS, SingleCellExperiment formats hub architecture using Seurat intermediate. Python dependency required.

title: scConvert: Universal Single-Cell Format Conversion for R — scConvert-package
text: scConvert uses following options control behavior, users can configure options: scConvert.dtypes.logical_to_int writing logical vectors, coerce integer types   ensure compatibility across languages (see BoolToInt   details) scConvert.dtypes.dataframe_as_group writing data.frames, always write group   regardless factor presence scConvert.chunking.MARGIN Default direction chunking datasets; choose : largest Chunk along largest dimension dataset smallest Chunk along smallest dimension first Chunk along first dimension last Chunk along last dimension  scConvert.dimreducs.allglobal Treat DimReducs global, regardless actual global status scConvert.compression.level Gzip compression level HDF5 dataset writes (0-9). Level 0 means   compression, level 4 (default) good balance speed vs size.   applies newly created datasets, data copied via   obj_copy_from.

title: scConvert: Universal Single-Cell Format Conversion for R — scConvert-package
text: Maintainer: Ziyu Zeng zzeng4@nd.edu (ORCID) [copyright holder]

what: Convert single-cell datasets between formats — scConvert
title: Convert single-cell datasets between formats — scConvert
text: Universal converter single-cell file formats object types. Supports arbitrary source/destination pairs routing Seurat hub format. Direct HDF5-level paths (h5ad <-> h5seurat) used available memory efficiency.

title: Convert single-cell datasets between formats — scConvert
code: scConvert(   source,   dest,   assay,   overwrite = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   standardize = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'character' scConvert(   source,   dest,   assay,   overwrite = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   standardize = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'H5File' scConvert(   source,   dest = "h5seurat",   assay = "RNA",   overwrite = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'h5Seurat' scConvert(   source,   dest = "h5ad",   assay = DefaultAssay(object = source),   overwrite = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   standardize = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'Seurat' scConvert(   source,   dest,   assay = DefaultAssay(object = source),   overwrite = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   standardize = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'loom' scConvert(   source,   dest,   assay = "RNA",   overwrite = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   standardize = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'SingleCellExperiment' scConvert(   source,   dest,   assay = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   standardize = FALSE,   ... )

title: Convert single-cell datasets between formats — scConvert
text: source Source dataset: Seurat object, SingleCellExperiment, loom connection, filename path, H5File connection dest Name/path destination file format. Supported formats: h5seurat, h5ad, h5mu, loom, rds. Also accepts "sce" return SingleCellExperiment object (-memory, file created). assay h5Seurat -> formats: name assay convert. formats -> h5Seurat: name assign assay. Default "RNA". overwrite Logical; TRUE, overwrite existing destination file. Default FALSE. verbose Logical; TRUE (default), show progress updates standardize Logical; TRUE, convert Seurat-style metadata column names scanpy/AnnData conventions converting h5ad format. example, nCount_RNA becomes n_counts, nFeature_RNA becomes n_genes. applicable conversions h5ad format. Default FALSE. ... Arguments passed specific conversion methods

title: Convert single-cell datasets between formats — scConvert
text: file destinations, invisibly returns destination filename.   dest = "sce", returns SingleCellExperiment object.

title: Convert single-cell datasets between formats — scConvert
text: Supported Formats: R objects: Seurat, SingleCellExperiment (requires     SingleCellExperiment), loom (R6 connection) File formats: h5seurat, h5ad, h5mu, loom, rds source format can converted destination format. Conversions without direct path go Seurat universal hub: Source -> Seurat -> Destination. Direct Paths (memory-efficient, full dataset loading): h5ad <-> h5seurat: Direct HDF5-level copy Key Features: Preserves expression matrices, metadata, dimensional reductions Visium/spatial data: reconstructs images scale factors Handles multiple data layers (V5 compatibility)

title: scConvert 0.2.0
text: Release Date: 2026-05-04

title: scConvert 0.2.0
text: HDF5 1.14+ required. SystemRequirements bumped HDF5 (>= 1.10.0) HDF5 (>= 1.14.0). hdf5r close path segfaults libhdf5 1.10.x closing files many open child IDs (groups/datasets opened via [[]] subsetting); upstream catchable R. Modern Linux distros (Ubuntu 24.04+, Debian 12+, Fedora 38+), recent macOS packages, Bioconductor’s Rhdf5lib ship 1.14+, matches real deployments.

title: scConvert 0.2.0
text: Native Stereo-seq GEF reader (LoadStereoSeqGef()). Pure-R reader BGI .gef .cellbin.gef files using hdf5r . Handles square-bin cell-bin schemas documented STOmics. Python, stereopy, reticulate dependency. Spot coordinates stored meta.data$spatial_x/y misc$spatial_technology = "StereoSeq". Native CosMx SMI reader (LoadCosMx()). Thin R wrapper around Seurat::LoadNanostring() validates canonical flat-file bundle (*exprMat*.csv, *metadata*.csv, *fov_positions*.csv, *tx_file*.csv) tags result misc$spatial_technology = "CosMx". squidpy reticulate dependency. CLI auto-delegation vendor raw formats. scconvert C binary now auto-detects .gef, .cellbin.gef, CosMx bundle directories transparently delegates R backend via Rscript + execvp(). Users can write scconvert mosta.gef mosta.h5ad directly. Rscript lookup happens -front; paths absolutised; shell-parsed command strings. FOV round-trip h5ad. writeH5AD() now serializes FOV@boundaries FOV@molecules stable uns/spatial/{library}/segmentation/ uns/spatial/{library}/molecules/ contract. readH5AD() automatically rebuilds FOV library finds load. Backward-compatible squidpy scanpy (ignore unknown uns/spatial/{lib}/ children). CLI varp preservation. new sc_stream_varp() src/sc_groups.c mirrors sc_stream_obsp() maps /varp/ (h5ad) /misc/__varp__/ (h5seurat). Handles sparse (CSR/CSC group) dense (array dataset) varp entries. Closes manuscript limitation “CLI preserve varp”. Loom factor-level preservation. writeLoom() now stores factor levels ordered flag /scConvert_extensions/col_factor_levels/{name}, outside col_attrs loompy/scanpy continue read file without errors. readLoom() restores factors load. h5mu per-modality uns round-trip. writeH5MU() readH5MU() now mirror modality’s uns group obj@misc[["__h5mu_uns_per_mod__"]][[modality]] per-modality uns entries survive h5mu round-trips instead flattened. IMC multi-image support. SeuratSpatialToH5AD() now iterates every image deterministic sorted order instead processing Images()[1]. Fixes IMC 14/15 -> 11/13 double-roundtrip degradation documented NOTES.md section 3.

what: P0 robustness fixes (Codex review response, Part A)
title: scConvert 0.2.0
text: SOMA / SpatialData generic dispatch. Lambdas registered scConvert generic now accept filename = scConvert.character() reaches right method. Adds tests/testthat/test-generic-dispatch.R. CLI build hygiene. CLI .o files isolated src/cli_obj/; make -f Makefile.cli install-bin copies binary inst/bin/scconvert; sc_find_cli() prefers source-tree src/scconvert. Bounded-memory R sparse streaming. .stream_sparse_group() now reads 64 MiB chunks (tunable via options("scConvert.stream_chunk_bytes")) instead materialising whole sparse matrices. Adds tests/testthat/test-stream-memory.R. Canonical h5mu layout write. writeH5MU() now writes top-level /var (concat modalities), /obsmap/{mod} (always 0..n-1 Seurat sources), /varmap/{mod} (block-diagonal -1 sentinels) muon convention. Atomic SOMA / SpatialData writes. writers now build sibling temp name rename success, mid-write crash leaves user’s existing path untouched. (writeSpatialData() initially shipped .exit deleted freshly-renamed output; fixed 2026-05-01 via write_succeeded disarm flag.) Python-validation tests CI. tests/scverse-env.yml + setup-micromamba make tests/testthat/test-python-validation.R runnable GitHub runner. Test longer hardcodes macOS conda path.

title: scConvert 0.2.0
text: C CLI memory-safety helpers. New sc_xmalloc(), sc_xcalloc(), sc_xrealloc(), sc_check_mul_size() src/sc_util.c replace raw malloc() calls overflow-checked allocations dense-embedding transpose sites src/sc_zarr.c:1043, 1668, 1674 column-buffer allocation src/sc_loom.c:138. Prevents SIZE_MAX overflow chip-scale embeddings. Defensive close_all wrap direct-path conversion. R/Convert.R wraps hfile$close_all() tryCatch HDF5 1.10.x graceful degradation. (1.14+ required tested; wrap -op .)

title: scConvert 0.2.0
text: Native Stereo-seq GEF reader (LoadStereoSeqGef()). Pure-R reader BGI .gef .cellbin.gef files using hdf5r . Handles square-bin cell-bin schemas documented STOmics. Python, stereopy, reticulate dependency. Spot coordinates stored meta.data$spatial_x/y misc$spatial_technology = "StereoSeq". Native CosMx SMI reader (LoadCosMx()). Thin R wrapper around Seurat::LoadNanostring() validates canonical flat-file bundle (*exprMat*.csv, *metadata*.csv, *fov_positions*.csv, *tx_file*.csv) tags result misc$spatial_technology = "CosMx". squidpy reticulate dependency. CLI auto-delegation vendor raw formats. scconvert C binary now auto-detects .gef, .cellbin.gef, CosMx bundle directories transparently delegates R backend via Rscript + execvp(). Users can write scconvert mosta.gef mosta.h5ad directly. Rscript lookup happens -front; paths absolutised; shell-parsed command strings. FOV round-trip h5ad. writeH5AD() now serializes FOV@boundaries FOV@molecules stable uns/spatial/{library}/segmentation/ uns/spatial/{library}/molecules/ contract. readH5AD() automatically rebuilds FOV library finds load. Backward-compatible squidpy scanpy (ignore unknown uns/spatial/{lib}/ children). CLI varp preservation. new sc_stream_varp() src/sc_groups.c mirrors sc_stream_obsp() maps /varp/ (h5ad) /misc/__varp__/ (h5seurat). Handles sparse (CSR/CSC group) dense (array dataset) varp entries. Closes manuscript limitation “CLI preserve varp”. Loom factor-level preservation. writeLoom() now stores factor levels ordered flag /scConvert_extensions/col_factor_levels/{name}, outside col_attrs loompy/scanpy continue read file without errors. readLoom() restores factors load. h5mu per-modality uns round-trip. writeH5MU() readH5MU() now mirror modality’s uns group obj@misc[["__h5mu_uns_per_mod__"]][[modality]] per-modality uns entries survive h5mu round-trips instead flattened. IMC multi-image support. SeuratSpatialToH5AD() now iterates every image deterministic sorted order instead processing Images()[1]. Fixes IMC 14/15 -> 11/13 double-roundtrip degradation documented NOTES.md section 3.

text: C CLI memory-safety helpers. New sc_xmalloc(), sc_xcalloc(), sc_xrealloc(), sc_check_mul_size() src/sc_util.c replace raw malloc() calls overflow-checked allocations dense-embedding transpose sites src/sc_zarr.c:1043, 1668, 1674 column-buffer allocation src/sc_loom.c:138. title: scConvert 0.2.0
text: readH5AD(): handle unsorted CSR column indices. scanpy-processed files scanpy.datasets.pbmc3k_processed() ship CSR matrices whose column indices sorted within row. Previously produced invalid dgCMatrix read. readH5AD() readH5MU() now detect condition via .sort_dgc_indices() R/LoadH5AD.R (sibling helper R/LoadH5MU.R) sort indices column-wise constructing sparse matrix. regression test added tests/testthat/test-regression-fixes.R. H5SeuratToZarr(): crash 1D dense datasets. direct h5Seurat -> Zarr converter previously attempted infer (rows, cols) 1D dense HDF5 dataset crashed dimensionality error. now emits warning skips offending dataset. Tracked “Chain D” benchmark manuscript; regression test included. writeZarr(): skip scale.data layer shape differs X. Seurat::ScaleData() produces (n_hvg x n_cells) matrix whose row count match X’s n_genes. AnnData requires layers/* match X’s shape, writeZarr() now skips layer whose dimensions differ default assay’s data matrix. regression test covers case. (See R/SaveZarr.R:131.)

title: scConvert 0.2.0

Added tests/testthat/test-stream-memory.R (bounded-memory verification tiny chunk budgets). Appended canonical-h5mu-layout block tests/testthat/test-h5mu-multimodal.R. Test suite now 166 test_that blocks 773 assertions macOS / Ubuntu / Windows (137 / 448 0.1.0). 19 vignettes build cleanly tools::buildVignettes() including 6 live Python interop chunks via reticulate.
